zh.desc: 本文提出了Reasoning Arena，一个自适应训练框架，旨在通过可验证奖励来提升强化学习的推理能力。该框架通过构建追踪比赛，比较不同的推理轨迹，从而生成更丰富的相对奖励信号。与传统方法不同，Reasoning Arena不再丢弃相似奖励的样本，而是利用动态更新的样本池进行高效的相对排名。实验结果表明，该方法在数学和编程基准测试中平均提高了7.6%的性能，并加速了训练过程。

papers.title: Light-WAM: Efficient World Action Models with State-Fusion Action Decoding
papers.abstract: Abstract Light-WAM is a lightweight world action model for robot manipulation that uses a compact video backbone and downsampled latent space for efficient future-video supervision, combined with a StateFusionActionExpert for direct action prediction.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct World Action Models (WAMs) extend robot policy learning by incorporating future prediction as an additional training objective, encouraging the policy to encode task-relevant temporal structure in its representations. Current WAMs often rely on large-scale generative architectures that incur high training costs and inference latency, making them difficult to deploy as efficient closed-loop policies. We propose Light-WAM, a lightweight World Action Model for efficient robot manipulation. Specifically, it is built with a compact video backbone and performs future-video supervision in a downsampled latent space, reducing the cost of video co-training while retaining its benefits for representation learning. For action prediction, Light-WAM introduces the StateFusionActionExpert, which reads adapted states from multiple backbone layers, fuses them through learned-query pooling, and directly predicts action chunks in a single forward pass. This design provides an efficient interface between video backbone representations and robot actions, avoiding the need for heavy generative action experts. Experiments demonstrate that Light-WAM maintains strong performance on LIBERO and achieves usable multi-task performance on RoboTwin 2.0, while using only 0.44B trainable parameters. It also achieves 72.03ms inference latency with 4.1GiB peak GPU memory and improved training throughput.

ru.title: Эффективное управление роботом через лёгкую модель мира и предсказание действий
ru.desc: Light-WAM — это облегчённая модель мира для манипулирования робота, которая использует компактный видео-кодер и сжатое латентное пространство для эффективного обучения с предсказанием будущих кадров. Модель вводит компонент StateFusionActionExpert, который объединяет признаки из нескольких слоёв кодера и напрямую предсказывает последовательности действий за один проход сети. В отличие от тяжёлых генеративных архитектур, Light-WAM достигает низкой задержки вывода (72 мс) и небольшого потребления памяти при обучении мировых представлений через вспомогательную задачу предсказания видео. Эксперименты показывают, что модель с 0.44 млрд параметров сохраняет сильную производительность на benchmark'е LIBERO и демонстрирует применимость к многозадачному управлению роботом.

en.title: Efficient Robot Manipulation with Light-WAM
en.desc: Light-WAM is a novel lightweight World Action Model designed for efficient robot manipulation. It utilizes a compact video backbone and a downsampled latent space to enable future-video supervision, which helps the model learn important temporal patterns for better action prediction. The model introduces the StateFusionActionExpert, which integrates information from various layers to predict actions directly, streamlining the process and reducing computational costs. Experiments show that Light-WAM performs well on benchmark tasks while maintaining low resource usage, making it suitable for real-time applications.

zh.title: 轻量级世界动作模型，提升机器人操作效率
zh.desc: Light-WAM是一种轻量级的世界动作模型，专为机器人操作设计。它使用紧凑的视频骨干网络和下采样的潜在空间，以实现高效的未来视频监督，并结合StateFusionActionExpert进行直接的动作预测。通过将未来预测作为额外的训练目标，Light-WAM鼓励策略在其表示中编码与任务相关的时间结构。实验表明，Light-WAM在保持强大性能的同时，仅使用0.44B的可训练参数，具有较低的推理延迟和更高的训练吞吐量。

papers.title: Answer Presence Drives RAG Rewriting Gains
papers.abstract: Abstract Controlled interventions reveal that gold answer presence in rewritten contexts significantly boosts QA performance, with removal causing substantial F1 drops and injection improving results, while conventional probing methods show fragility to sentinel changes.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Retrieval-augmented QA pipelines often route retrieved passages through an LLM rewriter before a smaller reader, lifting F1 by tens of points on multi-hop benchmarks; this gain is typically credited to improved evidence quality. We ask whether that lift is causally driven by the gold answer string appearing in the rewritten context rather than by curation per se, using a controlled intervention audit. For each rewritten context we re-run the reader after one of four controlled edits to the compile output: removing the gold answer span, replacing a length-matched random non-answer span (placebo), or injecting the gold into rewrites where it was absent (at the prefix or at a midpoint sentence boundary). Across twelve completed (cell, baseline) intervention runs spanning three reader families (Qwen2.5-7B, Qwen3.5-35B, GLM-4.7), two datasets (HotpotQA, 2WikiMultihopQA), and three compiler arrangements (MA-only, MB-only, MA+verify), removing the gold answer drops reader F1 by 28 to 64 points beyond the length-matched placebo on paired answer-in-compile strata, and prepending the gold into rewrites that lacked it raises F1 by +0.7 to +9.7 points in 10 of 12 (cell, baseline) combinations. A companion five-sentinel audit shows the conventional single-[MASK] probe is itself sentinel-fragile: on 2Wiki it reports a +4.12~F1 ``non-leakage residual'' that flips to -3.33 to -7.81~F1 under four alternative sentinels and fails an equivalence test for three of those four (1/4~pass). We do not propose a new rewriter or mitigation; we release the intervention runner and the sentinel panel so that other rewriter-gain claims can be tested against the same standard.

ru.title: Золотой ответ в контексте — главный виновник улучшения качества, а не переписывание
ru.desc: В статье исследуется, почему переписывание контекста улучшает производительность систем вопрос-ответ в многошаговых задачах. Авторы проводят контролируемые вмешательства, удаляя или добавляя правильный ответ в переписанный контекст, и обнаруживают, что присутствие ответа в контексте является главной причиной улучшения, а не качество самого переписывания. Дополнительно выявлено, что традиционные методы зондирования для оценки утечки информации оказываются неустойчивыми к изменениям способа маскирования токенов. Авторы предоставляют инструменты и методологию для проверки подобных утверждений о эффективности переписывания контекста.

en.title: Gold Answers: Key to Boosting QA Performance!
en.desc: This paper investigates how the presence of gold answers in rewritten contexts affects the performance of question-answering (QA) systems. The authors conduct controlled interventions to determine if the improvement in F1 scores is due to the gold answer being present or simply due to better curation of evidence. They find that removing the gold answer significantly decreases performance, while injecting it into rewrites enhances results. Additionally, they highlight the fragility of conventional probing methods, suggesting that their findings can help validate future claims about rewriter effectiveness.

zh.title: 金标准答案提升问答性能的关键
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了在重写上下文中金标准答案的存在如何显著提升问答（QA）性能。通过控制干预实验，发现去除金标准答案会导致F1分数大幅下降，而注入金标准答案则能提高结果。研究还表明，传统的探测方法对上下文的变化非常脆弱。我们提供了干预运行工具和哨兵面板，以便其他研究者可以验证重写增益的主张。

papers.title: Liberating LLM Capabilities in Full-Duplex Speech Models
papers.abstract: Abstract A text-first tri-channel speech interface enables real-time interaction with visible text output alongside spoken responses, demonstrating superior performance in full-duplex conversational tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Speech-based large language models are typically constrained to spoken replies, which limits their user-facing outputs to what can be verbalized and suppresses text-native capabilities such as code generation, structured analysis, and multi-step reasoning in realtime interaction, for tasks that require persistent, structured, and inspectable intermediate outputs. Existing work improves spoken reasoning or full-duplex turn-taking, but still treats text as a hidden intermediate state or a subordinate modality rather than a first-class output channel. We propose Listen-Write-Speak (LWS), a text-first tri-channel paradigm in which a single autoregressive LLM continuously listens to user audio, writes visible free-form text as its primary output, and speaks a realtime oral response in parallel under a shared causal attention context. This behavior is implemented entirely through a Token Schema, requiring no architectural modifications, and learned via a two-stage data pipeline that synthesizes per-second cognitive annotations consistent with the revealed input timeline. Empirically, LWS demonstrates strong full-duplex interaction on Full-Duplex-Bench, reaches 4.72 on VoiceBench AlpacaEval, achieves 92.6% writing-speaking consistency, and consistently outperforms its internal ablations on URO-Bench. These results suggest that visible writing can serve as a first-class output channel for speech interaction without sacrificing realtime responsiveness. The code and dataset are available on the project page: https://royalzhang.com/project/lws-page/.

ru.title: Текст как первоклассный выход в голосовых диалоговых системах
ru.desc: В работе предложена архитектура Listen-Write-Speak (LWS), которая позволяет языковой модели одновременно слушать речь пользователя, генерировать видимый текст и произносить речевой ответ в реальном времени. Основная инновация состоит в том, что текст рассматривается как полноценный канал вывода, а не скрытое промежуточное состояние, что позволяет использовать возможности, недоступные при чисто голосовых интерфейсах, такие как генерация кода и структурированный анализ. Реализация достигается через Token Schema без изменения архитектуры модели и обучается на синтезированных данных с временными аннотациями. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что LWS превосходит альтернативные подходы в полнодуплексном взаимодействии, достигая высокой согласованности между письменным и устным выводом.

en.title: Revolutionizing Speech Interaction with Text-First Outputs
en.desc: This paper introduces a novel speech interface called Listen-Write-Speak (LWS) that allows real-time interaction by combining spoken responses with visible text output. Unlike traditional speech-based models that only provide verbal replies, LWS treats text as a primary output, enabling complex tasks like code generation and structured analysis. The system operates using a single autoregressive language model that listens to audio, writes text, and speaks simultaneously, all while maintaining a shared attention context. Empirical results show that LWS achieves high consistency and performance in full-duplex conversational tasks, demonstrating the effectiveness of integrating text as a first-class output channel.

zh.title: 文本优先的语音交互新模式
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种文本优先的三通道语音接口，能够实现实时交互，同时输出可见文本和口头响应。这种方法称为Listen-Write-Speak (LWS)，它通过一个自回归的大型语言模型，持续监听用户音频，并将可见的自由文本作为主要输出，同时实时发声响应。LWS在全双工对话任务中表现出色，能够实现92.6%的写作与口语一致性，且在多个基准测试中超越了现有方法。研究表明，可见文本可以作为语音交互的主要输出通道，而不影响实时响应能力。

papers.title: A Geometric Account of Activation Steering through Angle-Norm Decomposition
papers.abstract: Abstract Research challenges the assumption that hidden-state norms carry concept-relevant information in language models, demonstrating that concepts are primarily represented in angular structure while norm remains crucial for steering stability and effectiveness across multiple models.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Linear activation steering has gained popularity as a simple and empirically effective way to control language model behavior. More recently, spherical steering paradigms have been proposed to address limitations of additive interventions, often motivated by the assumption that hidden-state norm does not carry concept-relevant information. In this work, we revisit this assumption through a controlled empirical study designed to disentangle the roles of angular and radial components. We show that steering methods differ mainly in how they couple two geometric effects: changing a token's angular alignment with a concept direction and changing its hidden-state norm. Across seven language models, we find that concepts are represented primarily in angular structure, supporting the motivation for spherical methods, but that norm remains important for the stability and downstream effects of steering. Our results explain why interventions with similar concept-level effects can behave differently, and suggest that activation steering should be parameterized by interpretable angular and radial components of the intervention, rather than by a single additive coefficient that entangles these two effects.

ru.title: Разделяй и властвуй: угловая и радиальная структура в управлении языковыми моделями
ru.desc: В этой работе исследователи изучают, как скрытые состояния языковых моделей кодируют концепции, разделяя информацию на две геометрические компоненты: угловую (направление) и радиальную (норма). Авторы показали, что концепции представлены в основном в угловой структуре скрытых состояний, что объясняет популярность сферических методов steering'а. Однако норма вектора остаётся критически важной для стабильности и эффективности вмешательств в поведение модели. Исследование рекомендует параметризовать методы активационного steering'а отдельно по угловым и радиальным компонентам, а не использовать один аддитивный коэффициент, который смешивает оба эффекта.

en.title: Understanding Concept Representation in Language Models: Angular vs. Norm
en.desc: This paper investigates how language models represent concepts, focusing on the roles of angular and radial components in steering methods. It challenges the idea that hidden-state norms are essential for conveying concept information, showing instead that concepts are mainly represented through angular structures. The study reveals that while angular alignment with concept directions is crucial, the norm still plays a significant role in ensuring stability and effectiveness across various models. The findings suggest that steering interventions should be designed with clear angular and radial parameters to better understand their effects on model behavior.

zh.title: 概念表示的几何结构与引导稳定性
zh.desc: 本研究挑战了隐藏状态范数携带概念相关信息的假设，表明概念主要通过角度结构来表示，而范数在多个模型中仍然对引导的稳定性和有效性至关重要。线性激活引导因其简单和实证有效性而受到欢迎，最近提出的球面引导范式旨在解决加性干预的局限性。通过控制实证研究，我们揭示了角度和径向成分的作用，发现引导方法主要在于如何耦合这两种几何效应。我们的结果表明，激活引导应通过可解释的角度和径向成分进行参数化，而不是通过单一的加性系数。

papers.title: DuMate-DeepResearch: An Auditable Multi-Agent System with Recursive Search and Rubric-Grounded Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract A multi-agent framework for deep research tasks that addresses planning, evidence acquisition, and report synthesis through decoupled components and dynamic optimization mechanisms.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Deep Research (DR) has emerged as a new agentic paradigm to tackle complex, open-ended research tasks, demanding systems that can iteratively frame problems, acquire evidence, verify sources, and synthesize long-form reports. In practice, however, current DR systems are constrained by four interrelated limitations: long-horizon planning over an underspecified scope, the bottleneck of decomposing and scheduling such tasks within a single agent, hallucination risk in long-form synthesis, and limited process auditability. This technical report presents DuMate-DeepResearch, a multi-agent DR framework built on the Qianfan Agent Foundry. The framework decouples the Agent Core, which handles task understanding, planning, and scheduling, from an extensible Tool Ecosystem for retrieval, evidence acquisition, and report rendering, making every intermediate decision and tool invocation explicitly traceable. Building on this infrastructure, DuMate-DeepResearch further introduces three mechanisms: (i) a graph-based dynamic planning strategy expands the research roadmap coarse-to-fine and continuously revises it through reflection, re-planning, backtracking, and parallel branching; (ii) a recursive two-level execution design delegates each complex search sub-task to an inner Search Agent that runs its own planning loop, isolating noisy retrieval and stabilizing long-horizon execution; (iii) a rubric-based test-time optimization mechanism dynamically generates task-specific quality criteria and uses them as live reasoning scaffolds for evidence-grounded synthesis and adaptive stopping. Across two deep research benchmarks, DuMate-DeepResearch establishes new state-of-the-art results: the best overall score (58.03%) on DeepResearch Bench, and the best overall score (61.95%) on DeepResearch Bench II while ranking first in information recall and analysis.

ru.title: Разложенная многоагентная система для умного синтеза научных отчётов
ru.desc: Статья представляет DuMate-DeepResearch — многоагентный фреймворк для выполнения сложных исследовательских задач, который разделяет функции между Core агентом (планирование и управление) и расширяемой экосистемой инструментов (поиск информации и синтез отчётов). Фреймворк использует три ключевых механизма: граф-ориентированное динамическое планирование с итеративной переработкой, рекурсивное двухуровневое выполнение с делегированием подзадач специализированному поисковому агенту и оптимизацию во время вывода на основе критериев качества. Система обеспечивает полную прозрачность всех промежуточных решений и вызовов инструментов, что снижает риск галлюцинаций при генерации длинных текстов и повышает надёжность долгосрочного планирования. Фреймворк достиг новых результатов на двух бенчмарках глубокого исследования, показав лучшую точность поиска информации и качество анализа.

en.title: Revolutionizing Deep Research with Multi-Agent Frameworks
en.desc: This paper introduces DuMate-DeepResearch, a multi-agent framework designed to enhance deep research tasks by addressing key limitations in current systems. It separates the core agent functions of task understanding and planning from a flexible tool ecosystem for evidence gathering and report creation, ensuring traceability of decisions. The framework employs a graph-based dynamic planning strategy for iterative problem framing and a recursive execution design to manage complex sub-tasks effectively. Additionally, it incorporates a rubric-based optimization mechanism to adaptively refine quality criteria during synthesis, achieving state-of-the-art performance on benchmark tests.

zh.title: 多智能体框架提升深度研究效率
zh.desc: 本论文介绍了一种名为DuMate-DeepResearch的多智能体框架，旨在解决复杂的深度研究任务。该框架通过解耦组件和动态优化机制，处理任务理解、规划和调度，同时提供可追溯的工具生态系统。DuMate-DeepResearch引入了图形动态规划策略、递归执行设计和基于标准的优化机制，以提高研究的效率和准确性。实验结果表明，该框架在多个基准测试中取得了新的最佳成绩，展示了其在信息检索和分析方面的优势。

papers.title: Text-to-Image Models Need Less from Text Encoders Than You Think
papers.abstract: Abstract Text-to-image models primarily utilize basic text representation aspects like word merging and order rather than complex contextual information encoded in full text embeddings.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Text-to-image models rely on text prompts as their primary interface to human intent. Prompts are encoded by a text encoder into embeddings that condition the image generation process. Beyond individual token meanings, text embeddings encode contextual information across the full prompt, such as compositionality and attribute binding. However, whether image models actually exploit this richer information remains underexplored. Here, we address the question: Which aspects of text representation are essential for image generation? We show that text-to-image diffusion transformer-based models commonly rely only on two relatively straightforward aspects of text representations: (i) the merging of adjacent tokens into a word representation, for words spanning multiple tokens, and (ii) word order, which is imprinted by the positional embedding of the text-encoder. To show this, we construct a new text embedding that encodes only individual word meanings and order but lacks any contextual information about the full prompt. We find that this bag of position-tagged words representation is sufficient to successfully guide image generation, achieving visual quality and text fidelity that are on par with full text embedding-guided generation. This demonstrates that, contrary to common belief, text-to-image models often do not use the rich information encoded in the text embedding beyond individual word meanings and word order. Instead, the decoding of complex linguistic structures is performed by the image model itself. Project webpage: https://nsping13.github.io/contextless-TTI/

ru.title: Текстовые модели генерируют изображения, игнорируя контекст промптов
ru.desc: Авторы исследуют, какую информацию из текстовых представлений действительно используют модели text-to-image при генерации изображений. Они обнаружили, что диффузионные модели на основе трансформеров полагаются только на два простых аспекта: объединение токенов в слова и порядок слов, закодированный в позиционных эмбеддингах. Исследователи создали новое представление текста, содержащее только значения отдельных слов и их порядок без контекстной информации об всём промпте. Результаты показывают, что такое упрощённое представление достаточно для качественной генерации изображений, что свидетельствует о том, что модели сами декодируют сложные лингвистические структуры.

en.title: Unlocking Image Generation: Simplicity Over Complexity in Text Representations
en.desc: This paper investigates how text-to-image models utilize text representations during image generation. It reveals that these models primarily depend on basic aspects like word merging and order, rather than leveraging the full contextual information available in text embeddings. The authors create a simplified text embedding that only includes individual word meanings and their order, demonstrating that this approach can still produce high-quality images. This finding challenges the assumption that complex linguistic structures are necessary for effective image generation, suggesting that the image model itself handles these complexities.

zh.title: 文本到图像模型的简化依赖
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了文本到图像模型在生成图像时对文本表示的依赖。研究发现，这些模型主要依赖于相邻词汇的合并和词序，而不是利用完整文本嵌入中编码的复杂上下文信息。作者构建了一种新的文本嵌入，仅编码单个词的含义和顺序，结果显示这种简化的表示足以成功引导图像生成。研究表明，文本到图像模型并未充分利用文本嵌入中丰富的信息，而是由图像模型自身解码复杂的语言结构。

papers.title: Trajectory-Refined Distillation
papers.abstract: Abstract On-policy distillation suffers from prefix failure where dense token-level supervision creates fragmented gradients; trajectory-refined distillation addresses this by correcting student rollouts at the trajectory level before distillation.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct On-policy distillation (OPD) has become a central post-training tool for large language models (LLMs), providing dense per-token teacher supervision along the student's own rollouts. In this work, we identify a common structural cause underlying OPD, which we call prefix failure. Under prefix failure, dense per-token supervision induces a bimodal teacher mixture and fragmented gradients that token-level loss truncation or reweighting fail to address. This observation motivates us to move beyond token-level loss interventions toward trajectory-level output corrections. We thus propose Trajectory-Refined Distillation (TRD), a trajectory-level correction method that revises the student's rollout under the teacher guidance while within on-policy support. By correcting problematic prefixes before distillation, TRD mitigates prefix failure at its source. Moreover, TRD improves the exploration by exposing the student to alternative valid derivations under teacher guidance, even when the original rolls are already correct. TRD can also be applied to on-policy self-distillation (OPSD), a parameter-sharing variant that uses the student model conditioned on privileged informations as the teacher. Across a wide range of benchmarks and base models at multiple scales, TRD consistently outperforms prior baselines, improving single-attempt accuracy and broadening reasoning coverage. Code is available at https://github.com/louieworth/trd

ru.title: Исправляйте ошибки на уровне траекторий, а не токенов
ru.desc: Работа раскрывает проблему 'prefix failure' при дистилляции моделей на собственных траекториях, когда плотный надзор на уровне токенов приводит к фрагментированным градиентам. Авторы предлагают метод Trajectory-Refined Distillation (TRD), который корректирует ошибочные префиксы на уровне целых траекторий под руководством учителя, а не пытается исправлять проблемы через перевешивание потерь отдельных токенов. Метод работает как с обычной дистилляцией, так и с самодистилляцией, и улучшает качество за счет использования альтернативных корректных решений при генерации. Экспериментально TRD показывает стабильное улучшение на различных бенчмарках и масштабах моделей.

en.title: Correcting Prefix Failures for Better Distillation
en.desc: This paper addresses a problem in on-policy distillation called prefix failure, where dense supervision at the token level leads to fragmented gradients. The authors propose a new method called Trajectory-Refined Distillation (TRD) that corrects the student's rollouts at the trajectory level before the distillation process. By focusing on trajectory-level corrections, TRD mitigates the issues caused by prefix failure and enhances the exploration of alternative valid outputs. The results show that TRD significantly improves accuracy and reasoning coverage across various benchmarks and models.

zh.title: 轨迹精炼蒸馏：解决前缀失败的创新方法
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了在政策蒸馏中存在的前缀失败问题，这种问题导致了密集的标记级监督产生了碎片化的梯度。为了解决这个问题，作者提出了一种新的方法，称为轨迹精炼蒸馏（TRD），它在轨迹级别上对学生的输出进行修正。TRD通过在蒸馏之前纠正有问题的前缀，从源头上减轻了前缀失败的影响。此外，TRD还通过教师指导让学生接触到其他有效的推导方式，从而提高了探索能力。

papers.title: SDR: Set-Distance Rewards for Radiology Report Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Set-based rewards using embedding distances improve chest X-ray report generation by enabling effective post-training and test-time selection without requiring causal reasoning structures.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards has rapidly advanced reasoning in vision--language models. However, for chest X-ray report generation, the standard rewards (i.e. exact-match accuracy and step-level processes) are incompatible because the reports consist of unordered and orthogonal findings, rather than a causal reasoning chain. We address this gap with a set-based view: each report is split into sentences and embedded by a frozen sentence transformer, yielding unordered embedding sets. We propose the use of set-to-set distances between generated and reference embeddings as continuous, permutation-invariant rewards. Across two datasets and three vision--language models (Qwen3-VL-2B/4B, Gemma3-4B), post-training with set-to-set distance based rewards via GRPO consistently outperforms supervised fine-tuning and exact-match GRPO on all headline metrics (BERTScore, RadGraph F1 and CheXbert F1 by average \%6.80, \%7.82 and \%4.45 relative improvements respectively). The same set distances also enable test-time best-of-N selection: scoring candidates by their distance to training-report embeddings outperforms random selection on our trained models as well as three closed-source LLMs (Mistral-Small, Gemini-2.5 Flash-Lite, GPT-4o-mini) with on average \%16.4 relative improvement on BERTScore. Used as a streaming signal, they support a more efficient form of test-time scaling: pruning low-scoring candidates mid-generation reduces generated tokens by over 50\% while preserving the Findings quality of full best-of-N selection. Together these results establish set-distance rewards as a unified signal for both post-training and test-time scaling in chest X-ray report generation. Our code is publicly https://anonymous.4open.science/r/Set-Distance-Rewards-CXR-BFDA{available}.
affiliations: Department of Biomedical Data Science, Stanford University School of Medicine
Department of Electrical Engineering, Stanford University
Department of Mathematical Modelling, Statistics & Bioinformatics, Ghent University

ru.title: Награды на основе множеств эмбеддингов для улучшения генерации медицинских отчётов
ru.desc: В статье предложен новый подход к обучению моделей зрения и языка для генерации отчётов по рентгеновским снимкам грудной клетки. Вместо стандартных наград, основанных на точном совпадении текста, авторы используют награды на основе расстояний между наборами эмбеддингов предложений, что лучше соответствует природе медицинских отчётов, состоящих из неупорядоченных и независимых находок. Метод позволяет проводить эффективное обучение с подкреплением через GRPO и отбор лучших вариантов на этапе тестирования без необходимости моделировать причинно-следственные цепочки. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительные улучшения на нескольких датасетах и моделях, а также позволяют сократить объём генерируемых токенов на 50% при сохранении качества.

en.title: Revolutionizing Chest X-Ray Reports with Set-Based Rewards
en.desc: This paper introduces a novel approach to chest X-ray report generation using set-based rewards derived from embedding distances. Traditional reward systems, which rely on exact matches, are inadequate for unordered medical findings, so the authors propose a method that treats reports as sets of sentences. By employing a frozen sentence transformer to create embeddings, they can measure the similarity between generated and reference reports using permutation-invariant distances. The results show significant improvements in report quality and efficiency during both training and test phases, demonstrating the effectiveness of set-distance rewards in this context.

zh.title: 集合距离奖励提升胸部X光报告生成
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种基于集合距离的奖励机制，用于改善胸部X光报告的生成。通过将报告分割成句子并使用冻结的句子变换器进行嵌入，生成无序的嵌入集合，从而避免了因果推理的限制。研究表明，基于集合距离的奖励在后训练和测试时选择中均优于传统的精确匹配奖励。最终结果显示，这种方法在多个指标上均有显著提升，证明了集合距离奖励在胸部X光报告生成中的有效性。

papers.title: Optical Reasoning: Rethinking Images as an Expressive Reasoning Medium Beyond Text
papers.abstract: Abstract Optical reasoning uses images as a standalone reasoning medium for language and multimodal tasks, achieving higher token efficiency than traditional text-based approaches.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Chain-of-Thought (CoT) improves the performance of Large Language Models (LLMs) and has been extended to Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). More recent work further moves from text-based multimodal reasoning toward interleaved-modal reasoning, where intermediate steps can incorporate both textual rationales and visual evidence. In this work, we propose a bolder and more ambitious idea: could images alone serve as the reasoning medium for both language and multimodal tasks? To explore this, we propose optical reasoning, which treats images as a standalone reasoning medium. We instantiate this concept with two variants: typographic-based optical reasoning, which optimizes visual layouts for compact rationale rendering, and graphical-based optical reasoning, which composes text and graphical elements into structured visual rationales. Across mathematical, scientific, and interleaved-modal reasoning benchmarks, optical reasoning can match or even exceed traditional text reasoning while reducing reasoning tokens by an average of 28.57% on language tasks and 16% on multimodal tasks, achieving 1.96 times the token efficiency of text reasoning. These results show that images can effectively and efficiently encode rationales while providing a unified visual canvas for reasoning.

ru.title: Изображения как эффективный носитель рассуждений вместо текста
ru.desc: В работе предложена концепция оптического рассуждения, которая использует изображения в качестве основной среды для логического анализа языковых и мультимодальных задач. Авторы реализовали две варианта: типографический (оптимизирующий визуальные макеты для компактного представления рассуждений) и графический (объединяющий текст и графические элементы в структурированные визуальные рассуждения). Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что оптическое рассуждение достигает производительности традиционного текстового рассуждения, при этом снижая количество токенов на 28,57% для языковых задач и 16% для мультимодальных задач. Данный подход демонстрирует, что изображения могут эффективно кодировать цепочки рассуждений, обеспечивая унифицированный визуальный интерфейс для анализа.

en.title: Unlocking Reasoning: Images as the New Language
en.desc: This paper introduces the concept of optical reasoning, which uses images as the primary medium for reasoning in language and multimodal tasks. By leveraging visual layouts and graphical elements, the authors demonstrate that images can serve as effective standalone rationales, potentially outperforming traditional text-based reasoning. The study shows that optical reasoning achieves significant token efficiency, reducing the number of tokens needed for reasoning tasks by an average of 28.57% for language and 16% for multimodal tasks. Overall, the findings suggest that images can encode complex rationales efficiently, providing a new approach to reasoning in machine learning.

zh.title: 光学推理：图像作为推理的新媒介
zh.desc: 光学推理使用图像作为独立的推理媒介，适用于语言和多模态任务，具有比传统文本方法更高的令牌效率。通过Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct链式思维（CoT）生成的内容提升了大型语言模型（LLMs）的性能，并扩展到多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）。本研究提出了一个大胆的想法：图像是否可以单独作为语言和多模态任务的推理媒介？我们的实验表明，光学推理在数学、科学和多模态推理基准测试中，能够匹配甚至超越传统文本推理，同时在语言任务上减少28.57%的推理令牌。

papers.title: Evaluation Cards: An Interpretive Layer for AI Evaluation Reporting
papers.abstract: Abstract AI evaluation results suffer from inconsistent reporting across platforms, prompting the development of EvalCards, an operational framework that standardizes benchmark metadata, evaluation data, and model information into a unified, interpretable record with four key interpretive signals.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct AI evaluation results are produced at scale but reported inconsistently across leaderboards, model cards, benchmark papers, and company blogs. The cost is interpretive: readers cannot reliably compare results across sources, identify what a report omits, or trace an aggregate claim to its underlying evidence. Recent efforts address isolated components but leave three gaps: they cover only narrow slices of the evaluation lifecycle and do not compose into a single interpretable record; they specify static representations that do not differentiate the questions different stakeholders bring to the same evidence; and they remain proposals on paper, lacking the extraction infrastructure required for adoption at scale. We present , an operational reporting layer that composes benchmark metadata, evaluation run data, and model metadata into a unified record. We (1) derive a reporting schema from a structured review of 52 papers and 10 stakeholder interviews, (2) implement four interpretive signals (reproducibility, documentation completeness, provenance and risk, and score comparability), rendered through reader modes calibrated to research and non-research audiences, and (3) deploy a monitoring tool that applies  across 5,816 models, 635 benchmarks, and 101,843 results, surfacing systematic gaps in current reporting practice.
affiliations: Quebec AI Institute, Université de Montréal
Weizenbaum Institute & Technical University of Munich

ru.title: Унификация отчётности об оценке AI через стандартизированные карточки EvalCards
ru.desc: Статья представляет EvalCards — операционный фреймворк, который стандартизирует отчётность о результатах оценки AI моделей, объединяя метаданные бенчмарков, данные экспериментов и информацию о моделях в единую интерпретируемую запись. Авторы разработали схему отчётности на основе анализа 52 научных работ и интервью с 10 заинтересованными сторонами, что позволяет решить проблему несогласованной публикации результатов оценки на разных платформах. В фреймворк встроены четыре интерпретативных сигнала: воспроизводимость, полнота документации, происхождение и риски, а также сравнимость оценок, адаптированные для разных типов пользователей. Инструмент был развёрнут в масштабе и применён к 5816 моделям, 635 бенчмаркам и 101843 результатам оценки, выявив систематические пробелы в текущей практике отчётности.

en.title: Standardizing AI Evaluation for Clearer Insights
en.desc: This paper introduces EvalCards, a framework designed to standardize the reporting of AI evaluation results across various platforms. It addresses the inconsistencies in how results are presented, making it difficult for users to compare and interpret data effectively. EvalCards compiles benchmark metadata, evaluation data, and model information into a single, interpretable record, enhancing clarity and usability. The framework includes four key interpretive signals to improve understanding for different stakeholders, ensuring that AI evaluation results are more accessible and reliable.

zh.title: 标准化评估，提升可比性
zh.desc: 本论文提出了EvalCards，这是一个标准化的评估框架，旨在解决人工智能评估结果在不同平台上报告不一致的问题。EvalCards将基准元数据、评估数据和模型信息整合成一个统一且可解释的记录，提供四个关键的解释信号。通过对52篇论文和10次利益相关者访谈的结构化审查，论文制定了一个报告模式，并实现了可重复性、文档完整性、来源和风险、以及分数可比性等四个解释信号。该框架已在5816个模型、635个基准和101843个结果中应用，揭示了当前报告实践中的系统性缺口。

papers.title: Phase Marginalization for Patch-Grid Instability in Vision Transformers
papers.abstract: Abstract Phase Marginalization is a post-hoc method that addresses phase-dependent instability in Vision Transformers by evaluating structured patch-grid phases and aggregating outputs in the original image coordinate system.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Vision Transformers operate on fixed patch grids, which can introduce phase-dependent instability for dense prediction: changing the patch partition can change the token evidence available to a pixel, especially near boundaries. We formalize patch-grid phase as a nuisance variable and propose Phase Marginalization, a post-hoc marginalization method that evaluates structured patch-grid phases, inverse-aligns dense outputs, and aggregates them in the original image coordinate system. The central variant, Uniform Phase Marginalization with K = 4, is training-free and improves over the canonical K = 1 baseline across measured segmentation, depth, and local matching settings. In a controlled Cityscapes experiment, Uniform Phase Marginalization provides a modest compute-matched advantage over generic shift-based four-forward test-time augmentation (TTA) (+0.31 mean Intersection-over-Union over the strongest tested generic row). A scaling study further shows that K = 4 is a practical cost-accuracy trade-off: K = 8 is essentially unchanged and K = 16 adds little accuracy at much higher latency. These results position patch-grid phase as a measurable nuisance variable and Phase Marginalization as a simple diagnostic and post-hoc marginalization baseline for dense ViT prediction.
affiliations: Scientific and Technological Research Council of Türkiye

ru.title: Маржинализация фазы сетки патчей для стабилизации Vision Transformers
ru.desc: Статья предлагает метод Phase Marginalization для решения проблемы нестабильности Vision Transformers, которая возникает из-за зависимости результатов от способа разделения изображения на патчи. Авторы формализуют фазу сетки патчей как мешающую переменную и применяют маржинализацию для получения устойчивых предсказаний плотных карт признаков. Метод работает в режиме post-hoc без дополнительного обучения и агрегирует выходы моделей при различных смещениях патчей в исходной системе координат изображения. Экспериментальные результаты показывают улучшение качества на задачах семантической сегментации, оценки глубины и локального сопоставления признаков с практичным соотношением вычислительных затрат и точности.

en.title: Stabilizing Vision Transformers with Phase Marginalization
en.desc: This paper introduces Phase Marginalization, a technique designed to improve the stability of Vision Transformers (ViTs) during dense predictions by addressing the issue of phase-dependent instability caused by fixed patch grids. The authors treat the patch-grid phase as a nuisance variable and propose a method to evaluate and aggregate outputs based on structured patch-grid phases, aligning them back to the original image coordinates. The method, particularly the Uniform Phase Marginalization variant with K = 4, is shown to enhance performance in segmentation, depth estimation, and local matching tasks without requiring additional training. Experimental results indicate that this approach offers a significant advantage over traditional test-time augmentation methods while maintaining a practical balance between computational cost and accuracy.

zh.title: 相位边际化：提升视觉变换器的稳定性
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为相位边际化的方法，旨在解决视觉变换器中的相位依赖不稳定性问题。该方法通过评估结构化的补丁网格相位，并在原始图像坐标系中聚合输出，来改善密集预测的效果。我们将补丁网格相位视为一个干扰变量，并提出了一种训练无关的均匀相位边际化方法，能够在多个测量设置中超越传统基线。实验结果表明，该方法在计算效率和准确性之间达成了良好的平衡。

papers.title: Robotic Policy Adaptation via Weight-Space Meta-Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract WIZARD is a weight-space meta-learning framework that generates task-specific LoRA parameters for frozen VLA policies using language instructions and demonstration videos, enabling efficient task adaptation without fine-tuning.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are emerging as a promising paradigm for robotic manipulation, enabling general-purpose policies trained from large corpora of demonstrations and action labels. However, adapting these models to new tasks still typically requires task-specific demonstrations, action annotations, and additional fine-tuning, making deployment costly and difficult to scale.   We propose WIZARD, a weight-space meta-learning framework that sidesteps task-specific fine-tuning by generating task-specific LoRA parameters for a frozen VLA policy. Given only a language instruction and a short demonstration video, WIZARD predicts the corresponding adaptation weights in a single forward pass, without target-task action labels or test-time optimization. During meta-training, WIZARD learns to map task evidence directly to expert LoRA updates, capturing relationships between tasks in weight space.   Experiments on LIBERO show that WIZARD improves performance by up to ~2x on unseen dataset collections and up to ~14x on unseen tasks. On a Franka Emika Panda, WIZARD consistently improves over a real-domain adapted baseline, showing that generated adapters provide task-level specialization beyond simulation.

ru.title: Одноходовая адаптация робота через генерацию весов из примеров
ru.desc: WIZARD — это фреймворк для метаобучения в весовом пространстве, который генерирует адаптивные параметры LoRA для замороженных VLA политик робототехники, используя языковые инструкции и видео демонстраций. Модель обучается отображать информацию о задаче непосредственно в обновления экспертных весов, позволяя адаптироваться к новым задачам за один проход без дополнительного обучения. Система не требует разметки действий на целевых задачах и оптимизации во время тестирования, что значительно упрощает масштабирование и развертывание робототехнических систем. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют улучшение производительности до 14 раз на неизвестных задачах и подтверждают эффективность в реальной робототехнической среде.

en.title: Efficient Task Adaptation in Robotics with WIZARD
en.desc: WIZARD is a meta-learning framework designed to enhance robotic manipulation by generating specific adaptation parameters for pre-trained models without the need for fine-tuning. It utilizes language instructions and demonstration videos to create task-specific LoRA parameters for frozen Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies. This approach allows for efficient adaptation to new tasks by predicting necessary adjustments in a single forward pass, eliminating the need for additional task-specific data. Experiments demonstrate that WIZARD significantly improves performance on both unseen datasets and tasks, showcasing its effectiveness in real-world applications.

zh.title: WIZARD：无微调的高效任务适应框架
zh.desc: WIZARD是一种权重空间元学习框架，能够为冻结的VLA策略生成特定任务的LoRA参数，从而实现高效的任务适应，而无需微调。该框架利用语言指令和演示视频，预测相应的适应权重，避免了对目标任务的动作标签或测试时优化的需求。通过元训练，WIZARD学习将任务证据直接映射到专家LoRA更新，捕捉任务之间在权重空间的关系。实验结果表明，WIZARD在未见数据集和未见任务上的性能提升显著，展示了其在真实环境中的有效性。

papers.title: AsyncWebRL: Efficient Multi-Step RL for Visual Web Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract AsyncWebRL improves vision-language web agent training through asynchronous reinforcement learning and trajectory normalization modifications, achieving faster throughput and better performance on challenging tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Training vision-language web agents with multi-step RL is compute-intensive, with two dominant forms of inefficiency: idle GPUs in synchronous RL, and trajectories that use more steps and tokens than necessary. We present AsyncWebRL, which addresses both. On the system side, an asynchronous design overlaps rollout, gradient update, and policy refresh across iterations, paired with two web-agent-specific adaptations, namely an everlasting rollout pool and lightweight screenshot handling, that together deliver up to a 2.9times end-to-end training-throughput speedup over the previously fastest open synchronous pipeline (WebGym). On the algorithmic side, we identify the per-trajectory normalizer 1/|τ_i| in multi-step GRPO as the root cause of trajectory-level and token-level inefficiency: because failures are systematically longer than successes, it down-weights the negative gradient on failed tokens, so the policy keeps producing verbose memory schemas. Replacing 1/|τ_i| with a constant 1/k breaks this coupling, contracting trajectories while preserving aggregate success. Together, these contributions set a new open-source state of the art on the WebGym out-of-distribution test split (+5.8% relative over the 42.9% prior best), with the largest gains on the harder slices (+42% relative on Medium, +48% relative on Hard).

ru.title: Асинхронное обучение для быстрого и эффективного обучения веб-агентов
ru.desc: Статья описывает AsyncWebRL — систему для обучения vision-language агентов, которые работают с веб-интерфейсами, используя многошаговое обучение с подкреплением. На системном уровне авторы внедрили асинхронное выполнение сбора опыта, обновления градиентов и обновления политики, что устраняет простой GPU и ускоряет обучение в 2.9 раза. На алгоритмическом уровне они выявили проблему в нормализаторе траектории в GRPO, который неправильно взвешивает отрицательные градиенты на неудачных действиях, приводя к излишне длинным траекториям. Замена переменного нормализатора на константу решает эту проблему и повышает эффективность обучения без потери качества.

en.title: Revolutionizing Web Agent Training with AsyncWebRL
en.desc: AsyncWebRL is a novel approach to training vision-language web agents using asynchronous reinforcement learning, which enhances efficiency by reducing idle GPU time. It introduces a system design that allows for overlapping tasks like rollout and gradient updates, significantly speeding up training throughput. Additionally, it addresses inefficiencies in trajectory handling by modifying the normalization process, which helps in reducing the length of unsuccessful trajectories. As a result, AsyncWebRL achieves state-of-the-art performance on challenging tasks, outperforming previous methods in both speed and accuracy.

zh.title: 异步强化学习提升视觉-语言代理训练效率
zh.desc: AsyncWebRL 是一种改进视觉-语言网络代理训练的方法，采用异步强化学习和轨迹归一化的修改，显著提高了训练速度和在复杂任务上的表现。通过异步设计，AsyncWebRL 能够重叠执行回合、梯度更新和策略刷新，从而实现高达 2.9 倍的训练吞吐量提升。算法方面，研究发现多步 GRPO 中的轨迹归一化器是导致效率低下的根本原因，替换为常数可以缩短轨迹长度，同时保持成功率。最终，AsyncWebRL 在 WebGym 的测试中设立了新的开源最佳表现，尤其在更难的任务上取得了显著的进步。

papers.title: WorldCraft: From Camera Navigation to Object Manipulation in Interactive Video World Models
papers.abstract: Abstract WorldCraft extends interactive video world models to enable object-level trajectory control while maintaining camera navigation capabilities through specialized control pipelines.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Recent video-based world models have made pixel-space environments interactive at the camera level: users can navigate viewpoints while the model generates coherent visual continuations. Yet their action spaces remain incomplete: users can move the camera, but cannot act on individual objects. Since real-world interaction is inherently object-centric, such models remain closer to passive scene observers than truly manipulable environments. We present WorldCraft, a framework that expands interactive video world models from camera navigation to object-level trajectory actions. Given a user click and a sketched path, WorldCraft generates future frames in which the selected object follows the prescribed trajectory while the camera continues to navigate the scene. WorldCraft achieves this through a trajectory-centric control pipeline: First, Normalized World Trajectory (NWT) represents user-drawn motion in a camera-invariant world coordinate system and dynamically re-projects it under the current camera pose, separating object motion from camera-induced screen-space displacement; Spatial-Pathway LoRA (SP-LoRA) then injects this world-space signal through the model's spatial-control pathway, adding object manipulation capability while preserving the pretrained camera controller; finally, Trajectory-Anchored State Persistence (TASP) treats the world trajectory as a persistent spatial state and refreshes autoregressive memory after trajectory-conditioned generation, allowing moved objects to reappear at their updated positions after leaving the camera view. Experiments show that WorldCraft enables accurate object control, preserves the video-based world model's camera fidelity under camera-only evaluation, and maintains object state across long autoregressive rollouts with off-camera excursions.
affiliations: AI Technology Center, Tencent Video, Tencent
The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: От пассивного наблюдателя к активному манипулятору: объект-центричное управление в видео-моделях мира
ru.desc: WorldCraft расширяет интерактивные видео-модели мира, добавляя возможность управления траекториями отдельных объектов наряду с навигацией камеры. Система использует специализированный конвейер управления, включающий нормализованные мировые траектории (NWT) для представления движения объектов в инвариантной к камере системе координат. Адаптация модели осуществляется через Spatial-Pathway LoRA, которая внедряет управляющий сигнал в пространственный путь нейросети, сохраняя при этом предобученный контроллер камеры. Trajectory-Anchored State Persistence обеспечивает согласованность состояния объектов при их перемещении за пределы видимой области, позволяя объектам правильно переpojавляться на обновленных позициях при возвращении в кадр.

en.title: Empowering Object Control in Interactive Video Worlds
en.desc: WorldCraft is a framework that enhances interactive video world models by allowing users to control the movement of individual objects while still navigating the camera view. It introduces a trajectory-centric control pipeline that separates object motion from camera movement, enabling precise object manipulation. The framework uses techniques like Normalized World Trajectory to represent user-defined paths and Spatial-Pathway LoRA to integrate these paths into the model's control system. This results in a system that not only maintains the visual coherence of the scene but also accurately tracks and updates object positions, even when they move out of the camera's view.

zh.title: WorldCraft：实现对象级轨迹控制的互动视频世界模型
zh.desc: WorldCraft 是一个扩展互动视频世界模型的框架，允许用户在保持相机导航能力的同时，对单个对象进行轨迹控制。通过用户点击和草图路径，WorldCraft 可以生成未来的帧，使选定对象沿着指定轨迹移动，同时相机继续在场景中导航。该框架采用了轨迹中心的控制管道，能够将用户绘制的运动与相机位置分离，从而实现对象的操控。实验表明，WorldCraft 能够准确控制对象，并在长时间的自回归生成中保持对象状态。

papers.title: Experience Makes Skillful: Enabling Generalizable Medical Agent Reasoning via Self-Evolving Skill Memory
papers.abstract: Abstract SkeMex is a self-evolving framework that enhances medical agents through structured skill memory, improving long-term clinical reasoning by distinguishing useful experiences and governing memory retention based on contextual utility.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Medical agent systems are increasingly expected to support interactive clinical decision making rather than only static question answering. In such settings, effective agents must reuse prior experience across evolving cases, yet existing memory mechanisms often retain raw historical traces that are redundant, noisy, and difficult to govern. More importantly, they rarely distinguish which memories are truly useful for future reasoning. This limits their ability to accumulate compact and reliable experience for long-horizon clinical reasoning. To close this gap, we propose SkeMex, a post-deployment self-evolution framework that improves medical agents through a skill-based memory without updating model weights. SkeMex distills informative interaction trajectories into structured skills that encode reusable procedural knowledge, and organizes them into a multi-branch repository spanning general, task-specific, and action-level experience. To determine which memories should be reused and retained, SkeMex estimates context-dependent utility from environment feedback and uses it to guide value-aware retrieval and repository governance. A closed-loop ``Read--Write--Assess--Govern" lifecycle further supports continual evolution by writing new skills, updating utilities, promoting useful memories, and removing harmful entries. Experiments across diverse clinical tasks show that SkeMex consistently outperforms representative memory-based agents in both offline and online settings. It also generalizes across model backbones and supports transferable skill memory. All data and code will be released publicly.
affiliations: Huazhong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Самоучащаяся система с умной памятью навыков для клинических решений
ru.desc: SkeMex — это фреймворк самоэволюции для медицинских агентов, использующий структурированную память навыков для улучшения длительного клинического рассуждения. Система отличает полезный опыт от шума в исторических данных и управляет сохранением памяти на основе контекстной полезности, не обновляя веса модели. Навыки организованы в многоуровневое хранилище, охватывающее общие, специфичные для задачи и действие-уровневые знания, с использованием обратной связи окружения для оценки их релевантности. Циклический процесс «Чтение—Запись—Оценка—Управление» обеспечивает непрерывную эволюцию агента путём добавления новых навыков, удаления вредоносных записей и повышения полезного опыта.

en.title: Empowering Medical Agents with Evolving Skill Memory
en.desc: SkeMex is a framework designed to enhance medical agents by improving their memory systems for better clinical reasoning. It focuses on creating a structured skill memory that helps agents identify and retain useful experiences while discarding irrelevant ones. By using feedback from the environment, SkeMex evaluates the utility of memories to ensure that only the most beneficial information is kept for future use. This approach allows medical agents to evolve continuously without needing to change their underlying model weights, leading to more effective decision-making in clinical settings.

zh.title: 自我进化的医疗代理框架
zh.desc: SkeMex是一个自我进化的框架，通过结构化的技能记忆来增强医疗代理，改善长期临床推理。它能够区分有用的经验，并根据上下文的实用性来管理记忆的保留。SkeMex提取有价值的互动轨迹，将其转化为可重用的程序性知识，并组织成多分支的经验库。通过环境反馈评估上下文相关的实用性，SkeMex指导记忆的检索和管理，从而支持医疗代理的持续进化。

papers.title: Hardening Agent Benchmarks with Adversarial Hacker-Fixer Loops
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers identify widespread vulnerabilities in agent benchmark verification systems and develop an automated iterative process using LLM agents to create robust verifiers that resist exploitation while maintaining legitimate task performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Agent benchmarks score submissions with outcome verifiers that are typically hand-written and brittle, leaving them open to reward hacking. We audit 1,968 tasks across five terminal-agent benchmarks and find 323 (16%) hackable by frontier models given only the task description. This corrupts both leaderboard rankings and RL training signal, yet the standard response is manual and reactive.   We introduce the hacker-fixer loop, a method for building exploit-resistant verifiers without per-task manual patching. The loop alternates three LLM agents: a hacker tries to pass the verifier without solving the task, a fixer patches the verifier to reject each discovered exploit, and a solver confirms the patched verifier still admits legitimate solutions. The loop iterates: each patch reshapes what the verifier rewards, surfacing the next exploit. We further add verifier access, and let patches transfer across tasks, to broaden the exploits the loop discovers.   On KernelBench, the loop drives the attack success rate from 62% to 0% on a held-out corpus of publicly reported exploits. We also find that weaker agents in the loop can defend against much stronger hackers: Gemini 3 Flash's loop drives the stronger Gemini 3.1 Pro and Claude Opus 4.7's attack success rate from 76% and 61% to 0% on KernelBench, and Gemini 3.1 Pro's from 39% to 17% on Terminal Bench across 77 tasks. We release Terminal Wrench (323 hackable environments, 3,632 hack trajectories) as a snapshot of the current attack surface, our patched verifiers, the exploits the loop discovered, and our implementation as a basis for future work.

ru.title: Автоматическая защита бенчмарков от манипуляций через итеративное взаимодействие агентов
ru.desc: Исследователи обнаружили, что системы верификации в бенчмарках для агентов уязвимы к манипуляциям и предлагают метод 'hacker-fixer loop' для создания надёжных верификаторов. Метод использует три LLM-агента: один пытается обойти верификатор, другой исправляет его уязвимости, третий проверяет, что легитимные решения всё ещё работают. Итеративный процесс повторяется, пока не будут закрыты все найденные эксплойты. На практике подход снижает процент успешных атак с 62% до 0% на тестовом наборе, и слабые модели эффективно защищаются от атак более сильных моделей.

en.title: Building Stronger Verifiers with the Hacker-Fixer Loop
en.desc: This paper addresses vulnerabilities in agent benchmark verification systems that can be exploited by advanced models. The authors propose an automated iterative process called the hacker-fixer loop, which uses three LLM agents to create robust verifiers that resist exploitation. The loop consists of a hacker attempting to bypass the verifier, a fixer updating the verifier to block the exploit, and a solver ensuring that legitimate solutions are still accepted. The results show a significant reduction in attack success rates, demonstrating the effectiveness of this method in enhancing the security of verification systems.

zh.title: 构建抗利用验证器的黑客修复循环
zh.desc: 研究人员发现了代理基准验证系统中的广泛漏洞，并开发了一种自动迭代过程，利用大型语言模型（LLM）代理创建稳健的验证器，以抵御利用，同时保持合法任务的性能。通过审计1968个任务，发现323个（16%）在仅提供任务描述的情况下可以被前沿模型利用。这种漏洞不仅影响了排行榜的排名，还影响了强化学习的训练信号。我们提出了黑客修复循环的方法，通过交替使用黑客、修复者和求解者三个LLM代理，构建抗利用的验证器，而无需对每个任务进行手动修补。

papers.title: PBSD: Privileged Bayesian Self-Distillation for Long-Horizon Credit Assignment
papers.abstract: Abstract Privileged Bayesian Self-Distillation enables fine-grained credit assignment in long-horizon tasks by converting sparse outcome rewards into calibrated turn-level signals through Bayesian evidence scoring and autoregressive decomposition.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Long-horizon agentic tasks pose a fundamental credit assignment challenge for outcome-base reinforcement learning: trajectory-level rewards verify final correctness but provide limited guidance on which intermediate reasoning steps or tool interactions contribute to the outcome. The difficulty is especially pronounced in multi-turn search agents, where successful trajectories may contain misleading actions and failed trajectories may contain valuable evidence-gathering steps. We propose PBSD (Privileged Bayesian Self-Distillation), a Bayes-calibrated self-distillation method for fine-grained credit assignment under sparse final rewards. PBSD measures trajectory quality through the posterior-to-prior probability ratio of the verified answer and applies Bayes' rule to convert this hard-to-estimate answer-side ratio into a tractable likelihood ratio between a standard student model and a privileged answer-conditioned teacher model. Autoregressive decomposition of this Bayesian evidence score yields turn-level signals that identify whether each intermediate turn supports or undermines the verified outcome. Consequently, PBSD provides a principled and elegant reweighting scheme that transforms sparse outcome supervision into Bayes-calibrated turn-level credit signals, while remaining fully compatible with standard policy optimization. Experiments demonstrate that PBSD consistently enhances performance across both in-domain and out-of-domain settings, and effectively transfers knowledge from short-context training to long-context inference, suggesting that its fine-grained credit assignment mechanism facilitates more effective policy learning and yields improved generalization.
affiliations: School of AI, Shanghai Jiao Tong University

ru.title: Байесовское самодистиллирование для точного приписания кредита в долгосрочных задачах
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод Privileged Bayesian Self-Distillation (PBSD) для решения проблемы приписания кредита в задачах с длинными горизонтами принятия решений в обучении с подкреплением. Метод преобразует разреженные финальные награды в калиброванные сигналы на уровне отдельных ходов, используя байесовское оценивание вероятностного отношения и авторегрессивную декомпозицию. PBSD применяет правило Байеса для конвертации сложно оцениваемого отношения правдоподобия между учительской моделью, обусловленной правильным ответом, и обычной ученической моделью. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что метод улучшает производительность как в стандартных, так и в новых условиях, и хорошо обобщается при переходе от обучения с коротким контекстом к выводу с длинным контекстом.

en.title: Transforming Sparse Rewards into Detailed Feedback with PBSD
en.desc: This paper introduces Privileged Bayesian Self-Distillation (PBSD), a method designed to improve credit assignment in reinforcement learning tasks that have sparse rewards. PBSD uses Bayesian evidence scoring to convert final outcome rewards into detailed turn-level feedback, helping to identify which actions contribute positively or negatively to the overall result. By applying Bayes' rule, it creates a likelihood ratio that allows for a more nuanced understanding of each step in a multi-turn process. The results show that PBSD enhances learning performance and generalization, making it easier for agents to learn from both short and long contexts.

zh.title: 特权贝叶斯自蒸馏：提升长时间任务的信用分配
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为特权贝叶斯自蒸馏（PBSD）的方法，旨在解决长时间任务中的细粒度信用分配问题。通过贝叶斯证据评分和自回归分解，PBSD将稀疏的最终奖励转化为可校准的回合级信号，从而提供更明确的指导。该方法通过比较验证答案的后验与先验概率，生成每个中间步骤对最终结果的支持或削弱信号。实验结果表明，PBSD在多种任务中均能有效提升性能，并促进了从短上下文训练到长上下文推理的知识迁移。

papers.title: OASIS: From Simulation Data Collection to Real-World Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
papers.abstract: Abstract A simulation-data-driven framework for humanoid loco-manipulation that uses 3D generative models to create realistic assets and hierarchical visuomotor policies trained on simulated data achieves better zero-shot performance than real-robot training.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Recent progress in robot manipulation has been largely driven by learning from large-scale demonstrations. For humanoid robot loco-manipulation tasks, however, existing data sources force an unsatisfying tradeoff between trajectory quality and scalability. Real-world teleoperation provides the highest-quality trajectories but requires dedicated physical space and time-consuming scene resets. Simulation offers an alternative way out of this dilemma: it can produce clean, embodiment-aligned data at scale without any physical hardware. In this paper, we propose OASIS, a simulation-data-driven framework for humanoid loco-manipulation. OASIS automatically reconstructs realistic object assets from real-world images using a 3D generative model. Based on these assets, trajectories are first collected through teleoperation in simulation, and then augmented under diverse domain randomizations in a post-processing stage. With the resulting simulation data, we further design a hierarchical visuomotor policy for humanoid loco-manipulation. Extensive experiments on the real humanoid robot show that, under zero-shot deployment, the policy trained on our simulation data achieves higher success rates on most tasks than that trained on real-robot teleoperation data, owing largely to the broad lighting and environmental variations covered by our simulation rendering, which real-robot data fails to capture. The project page is available at https://oasis-humanoid.github.io/.
affiliations: East China University of Science and Technology
Institute of Artificial Intelligence (TeleAI), China Telecom

ru.title: Симуляция вместо реальности: как генеративные модели улучшают обучение роботов
ru.desc: В этой работе предлагается фреймворк OASIS для обучения гуманоидных роботов задачам локомоманипуляции, который использует генеративные 3D модели для создания реалистичных виртуальных объектов из реальных изображений. Траектории собираются в симуляции через телеоперацию и затем дополняются разнообразной доменной рандомизацией для расширения обучающего набора данных. На основе этих симуляционных данных обучается иерархическая визуомоторная политика глубокого обучения. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что политика, обученная на симуляционных данных, достигает лучшей нулевой производительности на реальном роботе, чем модели, обученные на реальных данных телеопераций, благодаря лучшему покрытию разнообразия освещения и сцен.

en.title: OASIS: Simulated Success for Humanoid Robots
en.desc: This paper presents OASIS, a framework that enhances humanoid robot loco-manipulation by leveraging simulation data. It utilizes 3D generative models to create realistic object assets from real-world images, allowing for the generation of high-quality training data without the limitations of physical setups. The framework collects trajectories through teleoperation in a simulated environment and applies domain randomization to improve robustness. The results show that policies trained on this simulation data outperform those trained on real-robot data in zero-shot scenarios, highlighting the advantages of diverse training conditions.

zh.title: OASIS：模拟驱动的人形机器人操控新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为OASIS的框架，用于人形机器人在行走和操作任务中的应用。该框架利用3D生成模型从真实世界图像中重建逼真的物体资产，并在模拟环境中收集轨迹数据。通过对这些数据进行多样化的领域随机化处理，OASIS设计了一个层次化的视觉运动策略。实验结果表明，在零-shot部署下，基于模拟数据训练的策略在大多数任务上比基于真实机器人遥操作数据训练的策略表现更好。

papers.title: EmpiriGraph-Psy: A Dataset and LLM Pipeline for Extracting Empirical Relation Graphs from Psychology Abstracts
papers.abstract: Abstract Variable-centered empirical graph extraction maps psychology abstracts to typed graphs with normalized variables and empirical relations, achieving improved performance through staged pipeline approaches.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Existing scientific relation extraction benchmarks mainly target domains such as computer science, where entities are tasks, methods, datasets, materials, or metrics. This leaves a gap in variable-oriented empirical fields such as psychology, where findings are expressed as relations among constructs, measurements, interventions, and outcomes. We introduce variable-centered empirical graph extraction, the task of mapping scientific abstracts to typed graphs whose nodes are normalized variables and whose edges represent empirical and hierarchical relations. To support this task, we construct EmpiriGraph-Psy, a benchmark of 210 psychology abstracts annotated by domain-trained annotators with normalized variables, concept hierarchies, empirical relation types, and validation states. We evaluate frontier and open-weight LLMs using both direct extraction and a staged graph-construction pipeline that separates variable extraction, normalization, hierarchy construction, evidence selection, relation extraction, and edge validation. The staged pipeline substantially outperforms direct extraction, with the best configuration achieving a macro-F1 of 0.74. Error analysis shows that moderation relations and concept hierarchies remain the most challenging cases, highlighting the difficulty of extracting higher-order empirical claims and implicit abstraction structure from scientific abstracts.
affiliations: Department of Psychology, University of Warwick
Mathematical Sciences Institute, The Australian National University
School of Computer Science, University of Sheffield

ru.title: Поэтапное построение графов переменных для анализа психологических исследований
ru.desc: В статье предложен новый подход к извлечению информации из научных текстов по психологии - переменно-центричное извлечение эмпирических графов. Авторы создали бенчмарк EmpiriGraph-Psy с 210 аннотированными абстрактами психологических работ, где узлами графа являются нормализованные переменные, а рёбрами - эмпирические и иерархические отношения между ними. Они исследовали различные LLM модели, показав, что поэтапный конвейерный подход (разделение на этапы извлечения, нормализации, построения иерархии и валидации) значительно превосходит прямое извлечение, достигая макро-F1 = 0.74. Анализ ошибок выявил, что наиболее сложными остаются извлечение модерирующих отношений и неявных иерархических структур из научных абстрактов.

en.title: Transforming Psychology Abstracts into Structured Graphs
en.desc: This paper presents a new method called variable-centered empirical graph extraction, which transforms psychology abstracts into structured graphs. These graphs consist of nodes representing normalized variables and edges that illustrate empirical relationships between these variables. The authors created a benchmark dataset, EmpiriGraph-Psy, containing 210 annotated psychology abstracts to facilitate this task. They demonstrate that a staged pipeline approach for graph construction significantly improves performance over direct extraction methods, achieving a macro-F1 score of 0.74, while also identifying challenges in extracting complex relationships and hierarchies.

zh.title: 心理学摘要的变量中心图提取
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种变量中心的经验图提取方法，旨在将心理学摘要映射为带有标准化变量和经验关系的类型图。通过构建EmpiriGraph-Psy基准数据集，研究者们为210篇心理学摘要进行了注释，标注了标准化变量、概念层次、经验关系类型和验证状态。研究表明，分阶段的图构建管道在提取性能上显著优于直接提取，最佳配置的宏F1值达到了0.74。错误分析显示，调节关系和概念层次是最具挑战性的情况，突显了从科学摘要中提取高阶经验主张和隐含抽象结构的难度。

papers.title: Chiaroscuro Attention: Spending Compute in the Dark
papers.abstract: Abstract CHIAR-Former uses spectral entropy-based routing to dynamically select between DCT, RBF, and self-attention operators, achieving improved efficiency on large text datasets while maintaining performance through hybrid attention mechanisms.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Standard transformers apply self-attention uniformly at every layer and token, regardless of whether the input requires dynamic cross-token interaction. We propose CHIAR-Former (Chiaroscuro Attention), a 4-layer hybrid transformer that routes each token to one of three operators - DCT spectral mixing, RBF kernel mixing, or full self-attention - based on per-token spectral entropy, a theoretically justified complexity signal. Through systematic ablation on WikiText-103, we discover routing collapse: the router consistently rejects RBF in favour of DCT and attention, revealing that spectral mixing and dynamic attention are complementary and sufficient. A purpose-designed DCT+Attention-only variant achieves Val PPL 36.54 on WikiText-103 - a 45% improvement over a full-attention baseline (PPL 66.62) at 62.5% fewer attention FLOPs. We extend evaluation to WikiText-2, IMDB sentiment classification, and synthetic ListOps operations, establishing a clear operating regime: CHIAR-Former excels on large-scale naturalistic text where token diversity supports spectral specialisation, while full attention retains an edge on small datasets and synthetic pattern-matching tasks. These findings - both the wins and the losses - together define when and why spectral routing earns its keep.

ru.title: Интеллектуальная маршрутизация внимания через спектральную энтропию
ru.desc: CHIAR-Former - это гибридный трансформер, который использует спектральную энтропию для динамической маршрутизации каждого токена к одному из трёх операторов: DCT спектральному смешиванию, RBF ядерному смешиванию или полному self-attention механизму. Исследование показало, что маршрутизатор отдаёт предпочтение DCT и attention, отклоняя RBF, что указывает на взаимодополняемость спектрального смешивания и динамического внимания. На большых датасетах естественного языка модель достигает 45% улучшения в perplexity при снижении вычислительных операций attention на 62.5% по сравнению с базовым трансформером. Исследование определило оптимальные режимы работы: CHIAR-Former эффективен на больших текстовых корпусах с высокой разнообразностью токенов, в то время как полное внимание предпочтительно для малых датасетов и синтетических задач.

en.title: Dynamic Routing for Efficient Text Processing
en.desc: CHIAR-Former is a novel transformer model that enhances efficiency in processing large text datasets by dynamically selecting between different operators: DCT, RBF, and self-attention. It utilizes spectral entropy to determine the best operator for each token, allowing for a more tailored approach to attention mechanisms. The model demonstrates significant performance improvements, achieving a 45% reduction in perplexity compared to traditional full-attention models while using fewer computational resources. Through extensive testing, it is shown that CHIAR-Former performs best on diverse, large-scale datasets, while full attention is more effective for smaller datasets and specific tasks.

zh.title: CHIAR-Former：动态选择操作的混合变换器
zh.desc: CHIAR-Former是一种混合变换器，使用基于谱熵的路由动态选择DCT、RBF和自注意力操作符，以提高在大型文本数据集上的效率，同时保持性能。该模型通过每个token的谱熵信号来决定使用哪种操作，展示了谱混合和动态注意力的互补性。实验结果表明，CHIAR-Former在大型自然文本上表现优异，而在小型数据集和合成模式匹配任务中，全注意力机制仍然占优。通过这些发现，我们可以更好地理解谱路由的优势和局限性。

papers.title: Skill-3D: Evolving Scene-Aware Skills for Agentic 3D Spatial Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Skill-3D framework enables agents to learn scene-aware skills through self-evolving memory and skill libraries, improving tool utilization in 3D spatial reasoning tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct This paper explores agentic 3D spatial understanding, i.e., MLLM agents performing 3D reasoning through tool use. Existing methods often misuse tools and exhibit biased tool preferences under 3D scenarios, leaving the agentic paradigm with only marginal gains over non-agentic strategies. We reveal that 3D spatial reasoning tasks are heterogeneous across scenes, while these agents apply a uniform tool-use strategy to all scenes rather than selecting tools according to the specific scene and task. To address this, we propose Skill-3D, a framework that learns self-evolving scene-aware skills. Specifically, Skill-3D identifies the task scene and records the agent's tool-use trajectory into a Scene Memory, where successful trajectories from similar scenes are aggregated and distilled into a reusable scene-aware skill, with failed ones attached to the skill as lessons. During training, once a similar scene recurs, the corresponding skill is injected to guide the agent, producing new trajectories whose successes and failures further refine the skill, forming a loop in which the memory and the skill library co-evolve. Experiments show that Skill-3D substantially improves tool utilization in 3D spatial reasoning (from 39% to 78% on VSI-Bench), driving the agent toward correct and sufficient tool use. For instance, it improves Gemini-3-Flash by 67% on MMSI-Bench. Furthermore, we conduct agentic post-training over skill-guided trajectories, which boosts Qwen3-VL-8B by 43% on VSI-Bench.

ru.title: Обучение сцено-ориентированным навыкам через самоэволюционирующую память для пространственного рассуждения
ru.desc: В статье предлагается фреймворк Skill-3D, который позволяет агентам на основе мультимодальных больших языковых моделей (MLLM) обучаться осознанному использованию инструментов для трёхмерного пространственного рассуждения. Основная идея состоит в том, что агенты должны выбирать инструменты в зависимости от конкретной сцены и задачи, а не применять единообразную стратегию ко всем сценам. Фреймворк использует самоэволюционирующую память сцен и библиотеку навыков: успешные траектории инструментов из похожих сцен агрегируются и трансформируются в переиспользуемые сцено-ориентированные навыки, а неудачи сохраняются как уроки. В процессе обучения память и библиотека навыков совместно эволюционируют, улучшая качество использования инструментов с 39% до 78% на бенчмарке VSI-Bench.

en.title: Empowering Agents with Scene-Aware Skills for 3D Reasoning
en.desc: The Skill-3D framework enhances agents' ability to perform 3D spatial reasoning by developing scene-aware skills through a self-evolving memory system. It addresses the issue of agents misusing tools by allowing them to learn from their experiences in different scenes, creating a library of skills tailored to specific tasks. By recording successful and unsuccessful tool-use trajectories in a Scene Memory, the framework enables agents to refine their strategies over time. This leads to a significant increase in tool utilization efficiency, demonstrating the effectiveness of adaptive learning in complex 3D environments.

zh.title: Skill-3D：提升3D空间推理的智能体技能
zh.desc: Skill-3D框架使智能体能够通过自我演化的记忆和技能库学习场景感知技能，从而提高在3D空间推理任务中的工具利用率。现有方法在3D场景中常常错误使用工具，并表现出偏见的工具偏好，导致智能体的表现仅比非智能体策略略有提升。我们发现3D空间推理任务在不同场景中是异质的，而这些智能体对所有场景应用统一的工具使用策略，而不是根据特定场景和任务选择工具。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了Skill-3D框架，通过识别任务场景并记录智能体的工具使用轨迹，形成自我演化的场景感知技能。

papers.title: Where Rectified Flows Leak: Characterising Membership Signals Along the Interpolation Path
papers.abstract: Abstract Rectified Flows retain subtle training data traces that accumulate during training and can be exploited for membership inference attacks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Understanding what generative models retain from training data remains challenging, with implications for copyright and privacy. Beyond verbatim reproduction, models can encode subtler traces of their training data that never surface in their outputs yet remain exploitable. We study this regime for Rectified Flows, which are increasingly used in deployed generative systems. We analyse the interpolation path X_λ= (1-λ)X_0 + λX_1 that defines the Rectified Flow training. We show that a gap exists between the reconstruction of train and test data that follows a bell-shaped curve over λ, wich accumulates during training, while the validation metrics remain stable. The signal has a maximum whose location we derive in closed form under Gaussian assumptions. We validate these predictions on both audio and images and show that the bell-shaped structure is universal, while the peak prediction holds when our assumptions are satisfied. As a proof of concept, we exploit this specific λ-resolved structure to perform a Membership Inference Attack, distinguishing members of the training set from non-members.
