ru.title: Скрытые следы обучения в генеративных моделях: уязвимость Rectified Flows к атакам
ru.desc: В этой работе исследуются скрытые следы данных обучения в моделях Rectified Flows, которые накапливаются во время тренировки несмотря на стабильность метрик валидации. Авторы анализируют интерполяционный путь, определяющий обучение модели, и обнаруживают колокообразную кривую различия в реконструкции обучающих и тестовых данных в зависимости от параметра интерполяции λ. Они теоретически предсказывают положение максимума этой кривой при гауссовских предположениях и подтверждают универсальность наблюдаемой структуры на данных изображений и аудио. Как доказательство концепции, исследователи используют эту λ-разрешённую структуру для проведения атаки на определение принадлежности к обучающей выборке.

en.title: Unveiling Hidden Data Traces in Generative Models
en.desc: This paper investigates how Rectified Flows, a type of generative model, can unintentionally retain information about the training data, which can be used for membership inference attacks. The authors analyze the interpolation path used in training these models and discover a bell-shaped curve that indicates a gap in the reconstruction of training versus test data. This gap accumulates during training, even though validation metrics appear stable, suggesting that subtle traces of training data are preserved. The findings highlight the risks associated with generative models in terms of privacy and copyright, as they can reveal sensitive information about the training dataset.

zh.title: 揭示生成模型的隐秘数据痕迹
zh.desc: 本文研究了Rectified Flows模型在训练过程中保留的微妙数据痕迹，这些痕迹可能被用于成员推断攻击。我们分析了定义Rectified Flow训练的插值路径，发现训练数据和测试数据的重构之间存在一个钟形曲线的差距。尽管验证指标保持稳定，但这种差距在训练过程中逐渐累积。通过对音频和图像的验证，我们证明了这种钟形结构的普遍性，并利用这一结构进行成员推断攻击，以区分训练集的成员和非成员。

papers.title: SigmaScale: LLM Compression with SVD-based Low-Rank Decomposition and Learned Scaling Matrices
papers.abstract: Abstract SigmaScale learns auxiliary scaling matrices to improve truncated SVD-based LLM compression by adapting to individual weight structures through activation-aware transformations.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We present SigmaScale, a method for learning auxiliary scaling matrices S to aid truncated Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) based Large Language Model (LLM) compression. Instead of deriving scaling matrices analytically, SigmaScale optimizes two sets of vectors that define diagonal row and column scaling transformations under an activation-aware compression loss. We show that learned scaling lowers the effective intrinsic rank of weight matrices, as reflected by reductions in effective-rank entropy, and that this reduction is strongly correlated with compression loss. Experiments on Llama 3.1 8B Instruct and Qwen3-8B show that SigmaScale is competitive with closely related state-of-the-art SVD-based compression methods across perplexity and zero-shot benchmarks. By using learned activation-aware transformations, SigmaScale explores a more flexible route to low-rank LLM compression by adapting to the structure of individual model weights. The advantage observed in specific tasks makes our approach a valid option for applications requiring a reduced LLM-inference computing cost.
affiliations: Department of Computer Science, Aalborg University Copenhagen, Denmark
European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN), Geneva, Switzerland
INFN, Sezione di Genova, Genoa, Italy
MaLGa-DIBRIS, University of Genoa, Genoa, Italy

ru.title: Адаптивное масштабирование для эффективного сжатия LLM через обучаемые трансформации
ru.desc: SigmaScale — это метод сжатия больших языковых моделей, который использует обучаемые масштабирующие матрицы для улучшения разложения по сингулярным числам (SVD). Вместо аналитического вычисления масштабирований метод оптимизирует векторы, определяющие диагональные преобразования строк и столбцов, учитывая активации нейронной сети. Обучаемые трансформации снижают эффективный ранг весовых матриц, что коррелирует с качеством сжатия. Экспериментально показано, что SigmaScale конкурентен современным SVD-методам сжатия по метрикам перплексии и zero-shot бенчмаркам.

en.title: Adaptive Scaling for Efficient LLM Compression
en.desc: SigmaScale is a novel method designed to enhance the compression of Large Language Models (LLMs) using truncated Singular Value Decomposition (SVD). It learns auxiliary scaling matrices that adapt to the unique structures of model weights through activation-aware transformations. By optimizing these scaling matrices, SigmaScale effectively reduces the intrinsic rank of weight matrices, leading to lower compression loss. Experiments demonstrate that this approach is competitive with existing SVD-based methods, making it a promising solution for reducing computational costs in LLM inference.

zh.title: SigmaScale：灵活的低秩语言模型压缩方法
zh.desc: SigmaScale是一种学习辅助缩放矩阵的方法，旨在通过激活感知的变换来改善基于截断奇异值分解（SVD）的语言模型压缩。该方法通过优化定义对角行和列缩放变换的两个向量，而不是通过解析推导缩放矩阵。实验表明，学习到的缩放矩阵降低了权重矩阵的有效内在秩，并且这种降低与压缩损失有很强的相关性。SigmaScale在特定任务中表现出优势，使其成为需要降低语言模型推理计算成本的应用的有效选择。

papers.title: Cosine Misleads: Auxiliary Losses Reshape Vision Language Models, Not Their Latents
papers.abstract: Abstract Research challenges the conventional wisdom in latent visual reasoning by demonstrating that cosine alignment between supervised latents and visual targets negatively correlates with model accuracy, while revealing that answers are decoded downstream from latents rather than within them.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Latent visual reasoning (LVR) inserts supervised latent tokens between perception and answer generation in vision-language models (VLMs). The field uses alignment between these latents and their visual targets, i.e., cosine similarity or mean squared error (MSE), as both the training loss and the quality metric, assuming that better alignment yields a better answer. We test this with a designed matrix of five LVR variants and find the assumption inverted: cosine alignment is negatively correlated with accuracy across all five (r=-0.94). To explain this, we introduce PRISM, a pair of inference-time diagnostics: a linear probe that asks where the answer is decodable, and a corruption test that asks whether the latent is load-bearing. The supervised latents are largely bypassed. Corrupting them shifts accuracy by at most four points. The answer is decodable downstream of the latent but not at it, and the size of this decodability gap predicts how much each variant relies on its latent under perturbation. Consistent with an Information Bottleneck reading of the loss, the auxiliary objective reshapes the language model via shared parameters rather than via the latent variable it nominally optimizes.

ru.title: Латенты не содержат ответ: переосмысление визуального рассуждения в VLM
ru.desc: Исследование опровергает общепринятое мнение о латентном визуальном рассуждении в видео-языковых моделях, показывая, что косинусовое выравнивание между обученными латентами и визуальными целями отрицательно коррелирует с точностью модели. Авторы вводят PRISM — диагностический инструмент, который выявляет, что ответы декодируются не внутри латентов, а в слоях после них. Эксперименты демонстрируют, что вспомогательная объективная функция переформирует языковую модель через общие параметры, а не через латентную переменную. Исследование переосмысляет роль латентных представлений в информационных архитектурах моделей через призму теории информационного узкого места.

en.title: Rethinking Latent Visual Reasoning: Alignment Doesn't Equal Accuracy
en.desc: This paper challenges the traditional view of latent visual reasoning (LVR) in vision-language models (VLMs) by showing that better cosine alignment between supervised latents and visual targets does not lead to improved model accuracy. Instead, the research reveals that answers are derived from information downstream of the latents, indicating that the latents themselves are not crucial for decoding answers. The authors introduce PRISM, a diagnostic tool that assesses where answers can be decoded and whether the latents are essential for this process. Their findings suggest that the auxiliary objectives in training reshape the language model's performance without relying heavily on the latents, contradicting previous assumptions about their importance.

zh.title: 潜在视觉推理的新视角
zh.desc: 这篇论文挑战了潜在视觉推理的传统观念，表明监督潜在变量与视觉目标之间的余弦对齐与模型准确性呈负相关。研究发现，答案是在潜在变量之后解码的，而不是在潜在变量内部。通过设计五种潜在视觉推理变体的矩阵进行测试，结果显示余弦对齐与准确性之间的关系是反向的。引入的PRISM诊断工具表明，监督潜在变量在很大程度上被绕过，答案的解码主要发生在潜在变量的下游。

papers.title: Self-Evaluation Is Already There: Eliciting Latent Judge Calibration in Base LLMs with Minimal Data
papers.abstract: Abstract Self-Evaluation Elicitation (SEE) method improves model calibration for quality assessment through calibration-coupled reinforcement learning and masked distillation, demonstrating transferable quality evaluation beyond specific judge preferences.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large language models are increasingly evaluated by other models, raising a natural question: can a model predict how a judge will score its own output? We find that the ability is largely present before any targeted training: prompted few-shot, a base model already predicts an external judge's multi-attribute quality scores on open-ended responses well above chance across three benchmarks. We introduce Self-Evaluation Elicitation (SEE), a method that surfaces this latent ability through a short cycle comprising a calibration-coupled reinforcement learning phase that improves the answer and predicts the judge, followed by a masked distillation phase that sharpens the prediction while leaving the answer untouched. From 160 unique examples, roughly 31x fewer than a reinforcement learning baseline, SEE improves held-out calibration across three benchmarks while preserving answer quality. The elicited self-evaluation is sharply localized within the model's own token distribution and stable across judges it was never trained against, indicating a transferable notion of quality rather than a single judge's preference. These results reframe judge-aligned self-evaluation as a problem of elicitation rather than acquisition.

ru.title: Выявление скрытой способности моделей к объективной самооценке
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод Self-Evaluation Elicitation (SEE), который позволяет языковым моделям предсказывать, как внешний судья оценит их собственные выходные данные. Метод использует комбинацию калибровки, связанной с обучением с подкреплением, и маскированной дистилляции для выявления латентной способности модели к самооценке. SEE требует значительно меньше примеров для обучения (в 31 раз меньше чем базовый подход) и улучшает калибровку модели при сохранении качества ответов. Полученная самооценка стабильна и передаваема между разными судьями, что указывает на универсальное понимание модели качества, а не просто адаптацию к предпочтениям конкретного судьи.

en.title: Enhancing Model Calibration Through Self-Evaluation Elicitation
en.desc: The paper introduces the Self-Evaluation Elicitation (SEE) method, which enhances model calibration for quality assessment using calibration-coupled reinforcement learning and masked distillation. This approach allows models to predict how judges will score their outputs, demonstrating that this ability exists even before specific training. SEE effectively improves the model's predictions while maintaining the quality of the generated answers, achieving better calibration with significantly fewer examples than traditional reinforcement learning methods. The findings suggest that the model's self-evaluation capability is transferable across different judges, indicating a broader understanding of quality rather than being tied to individual preferences.

zh.title: 提升模型自我评估能力的创新方法
zh.desc: 自我评估引导（SEE）方法通过校准耦合的强化学习和掩蔽蒸馏，提升了模型的质量评估能力。该方法展示了超越特定评审偏好的可转移质量评估能力。研究发现，基础模型在没有针对性训练的情况下，已经能够较好地预测外部评审对其输出的多属性质量评分。SEE方法通过短周期的校准耦合强化学习和掩蔽蒸馏，显著提高了模型的自我评估能力，同时保持了答案的质量。

papers.title: Lean4Agent: Formal Modeling and Verification for Agent Workflow and Trajectory
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models can be equipped with formal verification frameworks using dependent-type languages to improve multi-step workflow reliability and performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Equipping Large Language Models (LLMs) to execute reliable multi-step workflows has become a central challenge in artificial intelligence. Despite recent advances in LLMs' agentic capabilities, most agent systems still lack formal methods for specifying, verifying, and debugging their workflow and execution trajectories. This challenge mirrors a long-standing problem in mathematics, where the ambiguity of natural languages (NLs) motivates the development of formal languages (FLs). Inspired by this paradigm, we propose **Lean4Agent**, to the best of our knowledge, the first framework that uses Lean4, a dependent-type FL to model and verify agent behavior. **Lean4Agent** launches **FormalAgentLib**, an extensible Lean4 library for formally modeling and verifying agent workflows' semantic consistency under explicit assumptions, and enabling localization of execution-time failures revealed by trajectories. Building on **FormalAgentLib**, we further develop **LeanEvolve**, which applies results in **FormalAgentLib** to revise workflows to enhance its capability. Extensive experiments on a hard problem subset of SWE-Bench-Verified and a subset of ELAIP-Bench across 5 leading LLMs indicate that the verification-passing workflows outperform the failing ones by an average of **11.94%**, and **LeanEvolve** further improves SWE performance by **7.47%** on average. Furthermore, **Lean4Agent** establishes a foundation for a new field of using expressive dependent-type FL to formally model and verify agent behavior.

ru.title: Формальная верификация поведения LLM-агентов через зависимые типы
ru.desc: В статье предлагается фреймворк Lean4Agent, который использует язык с зависимыми типами Lean4 для формальной верификации поведения LLM-агентов. Авторы разработали расширяемую библиотеку FormalAgentLib, позволяющую формально моделировать и проверять семантическую консистентность рабочих процессов агентов. На основе этой библиотеки был создан метод LeanEvolve для итеративного улучшения рабочих процессов с помощью результатов верификации. Эксперименты показали, что рабочие процессы, прошедшие формальную верификацию, работают на 11.94% лучше, а LeanEvolve дополнительно улучшает производительность на 7.47% в среднем.

en.title: Enhancing LLM Reliability with Formal Verification
en.desc: This paper introduces **Lean4Agent**, a framework that enhances large language models (LLMs) by integrating formal verification methods using a dependent-type language called Lean4. The goal is to improve the reliability and performance of multi-step workflows executed by LLMs, addressing the challenges of ambiguity in natural language processing. By utilizing **FormalAgentLib**, a library for modeling and verifying agent workflows, the framework allows for the identification and localization of execution-time failures. Experimental results show that workflows verified through this framework outperform those that do not undergo verification, demonstrating the effectiveness of formal methods in enhancing LLM capabilities.

zh.title: 用Lean4Agent提升大型语言模型的工作流可靠性
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为Lean4Agent的框架，旨在通过依赖类型语言来增强大型语言模型（LLMs）在多步骤工作流中的可靠性和性能。该框架利用FormalAgentLib库，能够正式建模和验证代理工作流的语义一致性，并定位执行时的失败。通过LeanEvolve，研究者进一步改进了工作流的能力，实验结果显示，经过验证的工作流在性能上优于未通过验证的工作流。Lean4Agent为使用表达性依赖类型形式语言正式建模和验证代理行为开辟了新的研究领域。

papers.title: PIPE-Cypher: Automatic Enterprise Benchmark Generation for Text-to-Cypher Systems
papers.abstract: Abstract A local benchmark-generation pipeline transforms live property graphs and seed queries into balanced NL-to-Cypher datasets for enterprise knowledge graphs, incorporating schema profiling, reverse-query grounding, and execution validation.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Enterprise property graphs vary widely in schema structure, internal terminology, domain assumptions, governance constraints, and user interaction patterns. A deployment-relevant Text2Cypher benchmark therefore reflects the questions users and agents actually ask of that graph. Creating such a benchmark is difficult because schemas and values are unique, and graph structure changes over time. Each NL-query pair must also be executable, use real graph entities, preserve diversity, and remain balanced across query types and difficulty levels. We present PIPE-Cypher, a local benchmark-generation pipeline that turns a live property graph and optional seed queries from customer questions, analyst logs, or agent tool calls into balanced NL-to-Cypher benchmarks. PIPE-Cypher combines schema profiling, reverse-query grounding, constrained generation, deterministic Cypher governance, execution validation, redaction, diversity controls, and a calibrated local LLM judge. Using local Qwen3.5-9B generation and judging, PIPE-Cypher exports 3,000 accepted FinBench/SNB examples, completes three audited ablation suites, calibrates judge behavior with human labels, and evaluates 11 local downstream models. The resulting benchmark is deliberately discriminative: zero-shot transfer is weak, while a few-shot control shows that schema-specific example banks can help compatible model families. Together, PIPE-Cypher makes Text2Cypher benchmarking a repeatable process that evolves with the graph, its users, and its target workloads.
affiliations: Halıcıoglu School of Data Science and Computing, University of California, San Diego

ru.title: Автоматическая генерация адаптивных бенчмарков для преобразования языка в граф-запросы
ru.desc: В статье представляется PIPE-Cypher — локальный конвейер генерации бенчмарков, который преобразует живые графы свойств предприятия и исходные запросы в сбалансированные датасеты для задачи Text2Cypher (преобразование естественного языка в запросы Cypher). Система использует профилирование схемы, обратное обоснование запросов, валидацию выполнения и локальную малую языковую модель для оценки качества. Авторы демонстрируют, что сгенерированные бенчмарки эффективны для оценки моделей, причём нулевой перенос знаний показывает слабые результаты, а few-shot обучение значительно улучшает производительность совместимых семейств моделей. Подход делает процесс создания специализированных бенчмарков повторяемым и адаптивным к изменениям графов знаний и требованиям пользователей.

en.title: Transforming Natural Language Queries into Cypher with PIPE-Cypher
en.desc: This paper introduces PIPE-Cypher, a pipeline designed to create balanced datasets for converting natural language (NL) queries into Cypher queries for knowledge graphs. It addresses the challenges of varying schema structures and user interactions by generating benchmarks that reflect real user questions. The pipeline incorporates techniques like schema profiling and execution validation to ensure that each query pair is executable and diverse. Ultimately, PIPE-Cypher aims to make the benchmarking process for Text2Cypher applications more systematic and adaptable to changes in graph structures and user needs.

zh.title: PIPE-Cypher：动态演变的Text2Cypher基准生成
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种名为PIPE-Cypher的本地基准生成管道，旨在将实时属性图和种子查询转化为平衡的自然语言到Cypher的数据集。该管道结合了模式分析、反向查询基础、执行验证等技术，以确保生成的查询对用户和代理的实际需求具有代表性。由于每个属性图的模式和结构独特，PIPE-Cypher能够处理这些变化并生成可执行的查询对。最终，PIPE-Cypher使得Text2Cypher基准测试成为一个可重复的过程，能够随着图的演变而不断更新。

papers.title: Set-Based Transformer for Atmospheric Compensation in Standoff LWIR Hyperspectral Imaging
papers.abstract: Abstract A lightweight deep learning framework is presented for atmospheric compensation in passive long-wave infrared hyperspectral imaging, enabling joint estimation of transmittance, atmospheric path radiance, and downwelling spectrum from multi-range radiance measurements.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Passive long-wave infrared (LWIR) hyperspectral imaging under a standoff geometry depends on atmospheric absorption and emission, as well as reflected radiance, thus making atmospheric compensation essential to get knowledge of a target of interest. Despite its importance, this compensation has been largely overlooked due to its practical and modeling difficulty. In this paper, we present a lightweight set-based deep learning framework that takes multiple radiance measurements, collected at different standoff ranges, as input and jointly estimates transmittance, atmospheric path radiance, and a shared downwelling spectrum. We analyze the learned representation with a sparse autoencoder and observe that several latent features do activate on geographically coherent subsets of the test data despite the absence of location supervision. Experiments on a MODTRAN generated standoff LWIR dataset demonstrate low spectral distortion across all estimated products. The dataset and code is publicly available at: https://factral.co/SAE-LWIR/

ru.title: Атмосферная компенсация гиперспектральных снимков через глубокое обучение
ru.desc: В работе предложена лёгкая нейросетевая архитектура для компенсации влияния атмосферы при пассивной гиперспектральной съёмке в дальнем инфракрасном диапазоне. Модель принимает радиометрические измерения с различных расстояний и совместно оценивает пропускание атмосферы, яркость атмосферного пути и нисходящий спектр излучения. Авторы проводят анализ выученных представлений с помощью разреженного автокодировщика и обнаруживают, что отдельные признаки активируются на географически связанных подмножествах данных без явного геолокационного надзора. Экспериментальная валидация на синтетическом датасете MODTRAN показывает низкие спектральные искажения во всех оценённых параметрах.

en.title: Revolutionizing Atmospheric Compensation in LWIR Imaging
en.desc: This paper introduces a lightweight deep learning framework designed for atmospheric compensation in passive long-wave infrared hyperspectral imaging. The framework processes multiple radiance measurements from different distances to jointly estimate key atmospheric parameters like transmittance and path radiance. It utilizes a sparse autoencoder to analyze learned representations, revealing that certain features correspond to coherent geographic areas without needing location data. Experimental results show that the framework effectively minimizes spectral distortion in the estimated outputs, making it a valuable tool for remote sensing applications.

zh.title: 轻量级深度学习框架实现大气补偿
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种轻量级深度学习框架，用于被动长波红外高光谱成像中的大气补偿。该框架能够从多范围辐射测量中联合估计透射率、大气路径辐射和下行光谱。尽管大气补偿在目标识别中至关重要，但由于实际操作和建模的困难，往往被忽视。实验结果表明，该方法在估计产品中具有低光谱失真，且数据集和代码已公开。

papers.title: CIPER: A Unified Framework for Cross-view Image-retrieval and Pose-estimation
papers.abstract: Abstract CIPER is a unified cross-view geo-localization framework that simultaneously performs city-scale retrieval and precise 3-DoF pose estimation using a shared transformer encoder and two-way pose decoder.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Cross-view geo-localization estimates the geographic location of a ground image by matching it against an aerial image database. Existing methods tackle this through either large-scale retrieval or precise pose estimation, but not both: retrieval-based methods enable wide-area search at the cost of localization accuracy, while pose estimation methods achieve high precision within only a narrow search space. Naively cascading these pipelines introduces error propagation and inconsistent feature representations. We formulate cross-view geo-localization as a unified problem requiring simultaneous city-scale retrieval and precise 3-DoF pose estimation. We propose CIPER (Cross-view Image-retrieval and Pose-estimation transformER), a single architecture that jointly performs both tasks through mutually beneficial feature learning. CIPER uses a shared transformer encoder with task-specific tokens to disentangle global retrieval features from spatial localization cues. To bridge the large domain gap between ground and aerial views, we introduce a two-way transformer pose decoder that uses ground features as spatial queries for bidirectional cross-attention. A set prediction strategy further enables stable 3-DoF regression under a unified multi-task objective. Experiments on VIGOR, KITTI, and Ford Multi-AV demonstrate competitive performance, especially under limited field-of-view and arbitrary orientation conditions. Code is available at https://github.com/yurimjeon1892/CIPER.

ru.title: Единая модель для одновременного поиска и точной локализации по кроссракурсным изображениям
ru.desc: CIPER — это единая архитектура для определения геолокации, которая одновременно решает две задачи: поиск изображений в масштабе города и точную оценку позы в трёх степенях свободы. Система использует общий трансформер-энкодер с задачеспецифичными токенами для разделения глобальных признаков поиска и пространственных сигналов локализации. Двусторонний трансформер-декодер позы применяет кросс-внимание для преодоления большого домена между наземными и аэрофотоснимками. Предложенный подход избегает распространения ошибок, которое возникает при каскадном соединении отдельных моделей, и демонстрирует конкурентоспособные результаты на нескольких датасетах.

en.title: CIPER: Unifying Retrieval and Pose Estimation for Accurate Geo-Localization
en.desc: CIPER is a novel framework designed for cross-view geo-localization, which means it helps determine the location of a ground image by comparing it to aerial images. Unlike traditional methods that either focus on broad searches or precise location estimates, CIPER combines both tasks into a single model, enhancing accuracy and efficiency. It utilizes a shared transformer encoder to learn features that are useful for both city-scale retrieval and precise pose estimation. By implementing a two-way transformer pose decoder, CIPER effectively bridges the gap between different image perspectives, leading to improved performance in challenging conditions.

zh.title: CIPER：统一的跨视角地理定位框架
zh.desc: CIPER是一个统一的跨视角地理定位框架，能够同时进行城市规模的检索和精确的三自由度姿态估计。它通过共享的变换器编码器和双向姿态解码器来实现这两个任务，避免了传统方法中常见的错误传播和特征表示不一致的问题。CIPER利用任务特定的标记来分离全局检索特征和空间定位线索，并通过双向变换器解码器来弥合地面视图和空中视图之间的差距。实验结果表明，CIPER在有限视野和任意方向条件下表现出色，具有竞争力的性能。

papers.title: Reasoning over Grammar: Can Synthetic Linguistic Reasoning Traces Enhance Low-Resource Machine Translation?
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models can improve translation for low-resource languages through structured linguistic reasoning traces, with the most significant benefits occurring during inference rather than training.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Large language models (LLMs) offer a promising approach to machine translation (MT) for extremely low-resource languages by incorporating linguistic resources through in-context learning. However, LLMs often struggle to apply grammatical information effectively during translation. Inspired by recent progress in chain-of-thought reasoning, we investigate whether low-resource MT can benefit from structured intermediate steps of linguistic analysis and grammatical reasoning. We propose a pipeline for automatically generating step-by-step linguistic reasoning traces from Universal Dependencies treebanks, dictionaries, and grammar-rule banks. We evaluate these traces in three settings: in-context learning (ICL), supervised fine-tuning (SFT), and reinforcement fine-tuning (RFT), on Xibe and Chintang as test cases. Our results show that linguistic reasoning traces are most effective as inference-time guidance: in ICL, reliable sentence-specific traces substantially improve translation performance across most models, languages, and metrics. In contrast, using the linguistic reasoning traces as training data yields smaller and less consistent gains, as models learn the trace format but often generate erroneous content. These findings suggest that LLMs can leverage grammatical information for low-resource MT when given reliable linguistic analyses, while learning to generate such analyses remains a major bottleneck.
affiliations: Center for Information and Language Processing, LMU Munich

ru.title: Лингвистическое рассуждение в инференсе: ключ к переводу малоресурсных языков
ru.desc: В работе исследуется, как большие языковые модели могут улучшить машинный перевод для малоресурсных языков, используя структурированные цепочки лингвистического рассуждения, основанные на данных Universal Dependencies и грамматических правилах. Авторы предлагают автоматически генерировать пошаговые трассы лингвистического анализа и грамматических рассуждений, которые затем используются при обучении с учителем и во время вывода. Результаты показывают, что эти трассы наиболее эффективны во время инференса через контекстное обучение: надежный лингвистический анализ значительно улучшает качество перевода на тестовых языках. Однако использование сгенерированных трасс как данных для обучения дает менее стабильные результаты, поскольку модели учатся форматировать вывод, но часто допускают ошибки в содержании.

en.title: Enhancing Low-Resource Language Translation with Linguistic Reasoning
en.desc: This paper explores how large language models (LLMs) can enhance machine translation (MT) for low-resource languages by using structured linguistic reasoning. The authors propose a method to generate step-by-step linguistic analysis from existing linguistic resources, which can guide the translation process. Their experiments show that these reasoning traces are most beneficial during inference, significantly improving translation quality when used in-context. However, the study also reveals that training models with these traces does not yield consistent improvements, indicating challenges in effectively learning to generate accurate linguistic analyses.

zh.title: 利用语言推理提升低资源语言翻译
zh.desc: 大型语言模型（LLMs）可以通过结构化的语言推理痕迹来改善低资源语言的翻译，尤其是在推理阶段的效果更为显著。研究表明，LLMs在翻译时常常难以有效应用语法信息。我们提出了一种自动生成逐步语言推理痕迹的管道，并在三个不同的设置中进行了评估。结果显示，可靠的句子特定推理痕迹在推理时显著提高了翻译性能，而作为训练数据使用时效果较差。

papers.title: Honest Lying: Understanding Memory Confabulation in Reflexive Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Agents relying on self-generated reflections can store confident but incorrect task interpretations, leading to persistent errors despite environment resets, which is identified through a new metric called Reflection Repetition Rate.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Reflexion-style agents rely on self-generated reflections as memory, implicitly assuming that agents can accurately diagnose their own failures. We show that this assumption can fail systematically: across ALFWorld and HumanEval, agents store confident but incorrect interpretations of the task and continue acting on them across trials, even though the environment resets to the correct task each time. We call this failure mode memory confabulation and introduce the Reflection Repetition Rate (RRR), a log-based metric that detects repeated reliance on incorrect reflective content. Using RRR, we identify 16 frozen environments in ALFWorld, where 0 of 121 reflections mention the correct target object, and 4 analogous cases in HumanEval. Our mitigation replaces open-ended self-diagnosis with programmatic extraction of trajectory-level failure signals, increasing correct object mention from 0% to 86%, reducing RRR from 0.64 to 0.10, and solving 3 of 16 frozen ALFWorld environments, suggesting that reflective memory can reinforce false beliefs rather than correct them.
affiliations: Department of Computer Science, University of Maryland Baltimore County, Baltimore, MD, USA

ru.title: Отражение ложных убеждений: как память агентов усиливает ошибки вместо их исправления
ru.desc: Статья исследует проблему 'конфабуляции памяти' в агентах, которые используют самогенерируемые размышления для обучения. Авторы показывают, что такие агенты могут уверенно запоминать неправильные интерпретации задач и упорно следовать им даже после перезагрузки окружения, вместо корректировки своего поведения. Для обнаружения этого явления предложена новая метрика Reflection Repetition Rate (RRR), которая отслеживает повторное использование некорректного рефлективного контента в логах агента. Авторы предлагают решение, заменяющее открытую самодиагностику на программное извлечение сигналов отказа из траекторий, что значительно улучшает производительность и снижает конфабуляцию.

en.title: Reflective Memory: A Double-Edged Sword in Task Interpretation
en.desc: This paper discusses how agents that rely on their own reflections can mistakenly hold onto incorrect interpretations of tasks, leading to ongoing errors even after the environment is reset. The authors introduce a new metric called Reflection Repetition Rate (RRR) to measure how often agents repeat these incorrect reflections. They demonstrate that agents can confidently misinterpret tasks, as shown in experiments with ALFWorld and HumanEval, where incorrect reflections persisted despite correct task resets. To address this issue, the authors propose a method that replaces self-diagnosis with systematic extraction of failure signals, significantly improving the accuracy of task interpretations.

zh.title: 反思记忆可能导致错误信念的强化
zh.desc: 本文探讨了依赖自我生成反思的智能体在任务理解中可能出现的错误。研究发现，这些智能体可能会存储自信但错误的任务解释，即使在环境重置后也会持续出现错误。我们引入了一个新的指标——反思重复率（Reflection Repetition Rate），用于检测智能体对错误反思内容的重复依赖。通过改进方法，我们能够显著提高智能体的正确任务识别率，表明反思记忆可能会强化错误信念，而不是纠正它们。

papers.title: Pruning and Distilling Mixture-of-Experts into Dense Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract A systematic framework converts mixture-of-experts models into dense architectures through expert scoring, selection, grouping, and knowledge distillation, achieving superior performance and efficiency compared to traditional pruning methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) is now the dominant architecture for frontier language models, yet it requires all expert parameters to be loaded in memory, making it less preferable for memory-constrained deployment. Existing compression methods reduce the number of experts but the output remains an MoE model with the same fundamental limitation. We present the first systematic framework for converting a trained MoE into a standard fully dense architecture: experts are scored, selected, and grouped, then concatenated into a dense FFN and refined by knowledge distillation from the MoE teacher. We evaluate 7 scoring, 5 grouping, and 2 magnitude scaling methods across a range of selected expert counts on Qwen3-30B-A3B, yielding 350 configurations. We find that the choice of scoring method is the most impactful, with our novel diversity-aware scoring consistently outperforming prior methods on Qwen3-30B-A3B, DeepSeek-V2-Lite, and GPT-OSS-20B. Under a controlled comparison at matched parameter count, MoE-to-dense outperforms dense-to-dense pruning by +6.3 pp in average downstream accuracy after ~4B-token distillation at 1.6x faster training wall-clock speed.

ru.title: Трансформация экспертных смесей в эффективные плотные нейросети
ru.desc: В статье предложена систематическая методика преобразования моделей архитектуры mixture-of-experts в плотные полносвязные сети через оценку, отбор и группировку экспертов с последующей дистилляцией знаний. Авторы разработали фреймворк, который оценивает 7 методов скоринга, 5 способов группировки и 2 метода масштабирования на примере Qwen3-30B-A3B, получив 350 различных конфигураций. Предложенный метод оценки с учетом разнообразия показывает превосходные результаты по сравнению с существующими подходами на нескольких больших языковых моделях. При сравнении при одинаковом количестве параметров преобразование MoE в плотную архитектуру демонстрирует преимущество в 6.3 процентных пункта над традиционным pruning-ом при 1.6x более быстром обучении.

en.title: Transforming MoE Models for Enhanced Efficiency and Performance
en.desc: This paper introduces a systematic framework that transforms mixture-of-experts (MoE) models into dense architectures, enhancing both performance and efficiency. The framework involves scoring, selecting, and grouping experts, followed by knowledge distillation to create a fully dense feedforward network (FFN). Unlike traditional pruning methods, which still retain the MoE structure, this approach eliminates the memory constraints associated with loading all expert parameters. The authors demonstrate that their novel diversity-aware scoring method significantly improves performance across various configurations, achieving better accuracy and faster training times compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 将混合专家模型高效转化为密集架构
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种系统框架，将混合专家模型（MoE）转换为密集架构。该框架通过专家评分、选择、分组和知识蒸馏，显著提高了性能和效率，优于传统的剪枝方法。研究表明，评分方法的选择对结果影响最大，新的多样性感知评分方法在多个模型上表现优异。经过对比，MoE转为密集架构在相同参数数量下，平均下游准确率提高了6.3个百分点，且训练速度提升了1.6倍。

papers.title: EMMA: Extracting Multiple physical parameters from Multimodal Data
papers.abstract: Abstract EMMA is a physics-informed multimodal framework that directly recovers dynamical parameters from raw video, audio, and image data using a Liquid Time-Constant network and physics-constrained loss.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce EMMA, a physics-informed multimodal framework that recovers all identifiable dynamical parameters of a system directly from raw video, audio, and image-based time-series observations. Unlike prior video-only approaches that struggle with occluded states, hidden actuation inputs, or assumptions about known initial conditions and coordinate frames, EMMA performs joint inference of explicit parameters, implicit dynamical components, and calibration invariants within a unified continuous-time model. EMMA leverages a Liquid Time-Constant (LTC) network to learn latent dynamics from heterogeneous modalities while a physics-constrained loss enforces consistency with the governing differential equations. A unified feature pipeline enables consistent alignment across video trajectories, acoustic signatures, and chart-derived measurements, allowing EMMA to estimate parameters under forced, implicit, and multivariate dynamics without requiring segmentation masks, differentiable rendering, or specialized sensors. Across 100+ scenarios including five standard dynamical benchmarks (75 Delfys videos), real-world rover and quadrotor systems with hidden inputs, and simulation-chart case studies spanning biological and chaotic systems, EMMA delivers robust multi-parameter recovery and significantly outperforms existing single-modality and equation-discovery baselines. Our results establish EMMA as a general, scalable solution for physics-consistent model extraction from opportunistic multimodal data. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/ImpactLabASU/EMMA-CVPR2026
affiliations: IMPACT Lab, School of Computing & Augmented Intelligence (SCAI), Arizona State University

ru.title: Восстановление физических параметров систем из мультимодальных данных с использованием физических ограничений
ru.desc: EMMA — это физически информированная мультимодальная система, которая восстанавливает динамические параметры физических систем напрямую из видео, аудио и изображений, используя сети с жидкостными временными константами. В отличие от предыдущих подходов, работающих только с видео, EMMA справляется с закрытыми состояниями и скрытыми входами, выполняя совместный вывод явных параметров и неявных динамических компонент в едином непрерывном временном пространстве. Система использует физически обоснованную функцию потерь, которая обеспечивает соответствие дифференциальным уравнениям, управляющим системой. На более чем 100 сценариях, включая реальные робототехнические системы и хаотические процессы, EMMA демонстрирует надёжное восстановление многих параметров и превосходит существующие методы анализа одной модальности.

en.title: EMMA: Unifying Multimodal Data for Physics-Informed Parameter Recovery
en.desc: EMMA is a new framework that uses multiple types of data, like video, audio, and images, to find important parameters of dynamic systems. It employs a Liquid Time-Constant network to learn from these different data sources while ensuring that the results align with physical laws through a physics-constrained loss function. This approach allows EMMA to work effectively even when some data is missing or hidden, unlike previous methods that relied on only video data. By testing on various scenarios, EMMA shows it can accurately recover parameters and outperform existing methods that focus on single types of data.

zh.title: EMMA：多模态数据中的物理一致性模型提取
zh.desc: EMMA是一个物理信息驱动的多模态框架，能够直接从原始视频、音频和图像数据中恢复动态参数。与以往仅依赖视频的方法不同，EMMA能够处理被遮挡状态和隐藏输入的情况，并在统一的连续时间模型中进行联合推断。它利用液态时间常数网络学习来自不同模态的潜在动态，同时通过物理约束损失确保与控制微分方程的一致性。EMMA在多个场景中表现出色，显著优于现有的单模态和方程发现基线，展示了其作为物理一致模型提取的通用解决方案的潜力。
